Unlike the more polished teen archetypes of the era, Leigh brought vulnerability and awkwardness to her character’s sexual awakening, setting herself apart as a performer unafraid to embrace discomfort. Throughout the 1980s, she gravitated toward emotionally and psychologically complex roles, often working under directors who pushed artistic boundaries. Her career choices reflected a dedication to the craft, earning her critical acclaim rather than tabloid attention. While many of her contemporaries pursued the limelight of the 'Brat Pack' fame, Leigh consistently chose roles that demonstrated depth and intensity, establishing

item.title: Morgan Fairchild’s Early Career and Television Roles in the 1970s
item.description: Morgan Fairchild, born Patsy Ann McClenny on February 3, 1950, is a renowned American actress who began her career in the early 1970s. She gained prominence as a glamorous television figure during the late 1970s, often portraying the sophisticated vixen archetype. Fairchild’s breakout role was as Jennifer Pace on the CBS soap opera 'Search for Tomorrow' from 1973 to 1977, where she portrayed a complex socialite whose performance established her reputation as an actress capable of playing the woman viewers loved to hate. After relocating to Los Angeles, she became a frequent presence on primetime television, including her role as the original Jenna Wade in 'Dallas' in 1978, which, despite being brief, left a lasting impression. Additionally, she held a recurring role on the popular sitcom 'Mork & Mindy' from 1978 to 1979 and frequently appeared on shows like 'The Love Boat' and 'Fantasy Island,' typically as wealthy or fashionable characters. Towards the end of the decade, Fairchild sta

item.title: Rosenberg Children Confront Parents’ Execution and Life After Orphanhood
item.description: On June 19, 1953, Michael (10) and Robert (6) Rosenberg faced the grim news that their parents, Julius and Ethel Rosenberg, were scheduled to be executed the following day for conspiracy to commit espionage, accused of passing atomic secrets to the Soviet Union during the Cold War. Photographed reading this devastating news while staying at a friend's home in Toms River, New Jersey, the boys were left orphaned after the execution. Shunned by much of their extended family due to the stigma surrounding the Rosenberg name, the children were eventually adopted by Abel and Anne Meeropol. Abel, known for his powerful anti-lynching song “Strange Fruit,” provided the boys with a nurturing home that emphasized intellectual growth and social awareness. Taking the Meeropol surname, Michael became an economist and professor, while Robert pursued law and later founded the Rosenberg Fund for Children, supporting children of persecuted activists. As adults, both brothers have engaged with the histori

item.title: Early Career Portraits of Ida Lupino Highlighting Her Role in ‘Come On, Marines!’ (1934)
item.description: In 1934, 16-year-old British-born actress Ida Lupino starred as Esther Smith-Hamilton in the pre-Code comedy-adventure “Come On, Marines!” produced by Paramount Pictures. The film follows a squad of U.S. Marines sent to the Philippine jungle to rescue what they believe are shipwrecked children, only to discover a group of young women instead. Lupino plays the leader of this shipwrecked “beauty chorus” and serves as the romantic interest for Sergeant “Lucky” Davis, portrayed by Richard Arlen. At this early stage, Paramount promoted her as “The English Jean Harlow,” emphasizing her platinum blonde hairstyle and elegant costumes, which remain impeccably styled even in jungle settings. The movie also features future star Ann Sheridan, credited as Clara Lou Sheridan, and Toby Wing. Despite the adventurous premise, the jungle scenes were filmed on studio sets and Sherwood Lake, keeping the women in glamorous attire while the Marines appear muddy and disheveled. Although “Come On, Marines!” i

item.title: Ford’s 1960s “Tot-Guard”: An Early Attempt at Child Car Safety
item.description: In the late 1960s, Ford Motor Company introduced the “Tot-Guard,” an innovative child safety car seat designed to keep young children secured during car rides. Weighing only five pounds, the Tot-Guard featured a molded seat base and a hollow polyethylene body shield that enclosed the child’s torso and legs, which was then secured using the vehicle’s standard lap belt. This design allowed some movement inside the shield while attempting to protect the child in case of a collision by distributing impact forces over a wider area. Ford engineers tested the system extensively, claiming children could remain comfortable for up to four hours. Despite its creativity, the Tot-Guard reflected the less rigorous safety standards of its time and is considered unsafe by today’s regulations. Modern car seats now use advanced materials, five-point harnesses, and rigorous crash testing. The Tot-Guard was forward-facing only and relied solely on a lap belt for restraint, making it obsolete and unsafe fo

item.title: MIT Study Finds Cysteine Promotes Intestinal Stem Cell Regeneration
item.description: Researchers at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology have identified cysteine, a naturally occurring amino acid present in protein-rich foods like meat, dairy, beans, and nuts, as a powerful stimulator of intestinal repair. In mouse studies, a cysteine-rich diet triggered immune cells called CD8 T cells to produce IL-22, a cytokine that activates intestinal stem cells and promotes tissue regeneration. This process helps the gut recover from damage caused by radiation and chemotherapy. The effect is strongest in the small intestine, where most dietary protein is absorbed. While the human body can produce cysteine from methionine, dietary cysteine delivers a more direct impact on the gut. The research represents the first time a single nutrient has been shown to directly enhance intestinal stem cell regeneration, opening potential avenues for dietary therapies that could help cancer patients recover from treatment-related gut injuries. The MIT team is also exploring whether cysteine

item.title: Swans and Collingwood Coaches Differ on Player Interaction During Matches
item.description: During a recent AFL match, Collingwood coach Craig McRae and Sydney Swans coach Dean Cox highlighted differing views on interactions with opposing players. McRae, who has a history of animated boundary-line coaching, defended his in-game interaction with former Magpies player Brodie Grundy, stating that his established relationship with Grundy makes such exchanges acceptable. Despite media attention and criticism, McRae insisted he would continue coaching from the boundary, valuing the energy it provides and believing in the appropriateness of interaction when a strong relationship exists. Cox, on the other hand, emphasized that neither he nor his staff engage with opposing players during matches, citing it as a matter of personal and team philosophy rather than an official policy. Additionally, the article covered Essendon coach Brad Scott dismissing external concerns over his future amid the team's losing streak, highlighting the focus on preparing players for the week’s match. AFLW’

item.title: Jackie Stoloff: Iconic Model and Muse of 1950s Fashion
item.description: Jacqueline “Jackie” Stoloff was one of the most influential models of the late 1940s and early 1950s, representing the elegant transition from post-war fashion to the youthful, dynamic styles of the era. Known for her distinctive bone structure, graceful poise, and ability to embody both the sophisticated 'New Look' and emerging modern trends, Stoloff became a muse for top designers and photographers. Her work appeared frequently in Vogue and Harper's Bazaar, and she collaborated with iconic photographers such as Richard Avedon, Georges Saad, and Philippe Pottier. Throughout her career, Stoloff modeled pieces from legendary designers including Jacques Fath, Christian Dior, Balenciaga, Jeanne Paquin, and Elsa Schiaparelli. She was celebrated not just as a model, but as a symbol of refinement and quiet intelligence, leaving a lasting impact on the visual culture of mid-20th-century fashion. Her photographs capture an era of meticulous tailoring, luxurious fabrics, and innovative designs,

item.title: Israeli Security Minister Criticized for Handling of Gaza Flotilla Detainees
item.description: Israeli Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir has faced international condemnation following the release of a video showing him appearing to take pleasure in the detention of activists aboard a Gaza-bound flotilla. In the footage, a woman shouting slogans in support of Palestine is forcibly restrained by an Israeli security officer while Ben-Gvir is seen smiling. The video, shared publicly by Ben-Gvir, has sparked outrage not only from human rights groups but also from allied governments concerned about Israel's treatment of civilians during protests and maritime operations. The incident highlights the ongoing tensions surrounding Gaza, where humanitarian aid efforts and political activism are often met with strong security measures. Critics argue that the minister’s behavior undermines Israel’s diplomatic relations and raises serious ethical and legal questions about the treatment of activists, particularly those advocating for Palestinian rights. This episode comes amid heightened scruti

item.title: Laika Unveils 'Wildwood', a New Stop-Motion Adventure Set in Portland
item.description: Laika, the renowned stop-motion animation studio, returns with its newest film, 'Wildwood,' marking seven years since their last feature, 'The Missing Link.' Known for creating intricately crafted, tangible worlds, Laika continues to push the boundaries of stop-motion animation. 'Wildwood' follows teenager Prue McKeel, who ventures into a secret wilderness beyond Portland to rescue her kidnapped baby brother, encountering magical and mysterious elements along the way. The film is directed by Travis Knight, who draws on his Portland upbringing to merge real locations like the Hawthorne Bridge with fantastical landscapes. The story, adapted by Chris Butler from Colin Meloy’s book series, promises themes of love, loss, and discovery. The score is composed by M83, adding a rich musical layer to the visual storytelling. Notable voice actors including Carey Mulligan, Mahershala Ali, Tom Waits, Awkwafina, Jacob Tremblay, Angela Bassett, and Jemaine Clement bring the characters to life. 'Wildw

item.title: How Switching to a Custom Payment Gateway Boosted Our E-Commerce Success
item.description: The article discusses the challenges faced by businesses restricted by traditional e-commerce platforms. The author recounts their experience building a store on Shopify, only to discover that platform-imposed limitations prevented access to certain payment gateways. Initial attempts to use alternative payment methods, including cryptocurrencies, or integrate third-party gateways proved either impractical or overly complex. The breakthrough came when the team adopted Unchained Commerce, a customizable, API-based payment processing library. By building a custom payment gateway, they improved payment processing rates by 30%, halved transaction failures, reduced latency from 500ms to 150ms, and decreased memory usage by 20%. Crucially, this change enabled businesses in previously blocked countries to access their services. The author reflects that a more modular architecture from the beginning could have provided even greater flexibility. The story emphasizes the importance of adapting te

item.title: HONOR 600 Series Launches in the Philippines with Advanced AI Features
item.description: HONOR Philippines recently unveiled the HONOR 600 Series in a high-profile launch event in Manila, highlighting the brand's focus on innovation and AI-powered mobile technology. The event featured notable personalities, including Miss Universe Philippines 2026 Bea Millan-Windorski, and performances by local artists ALAMAT and Wilbert Ross, emphasizing HONOR's support for Filipino talent. Central to the series is the AI Image-to-Video 2.0 technology, which allows users to transform still images into lifelike videos using up to three images combined with natural language prompts, offering creators a powerful tool for mobile content production. The devices boast advanced camera systems, intelligent performance features, and a sleek design aimed at a wide range of users. Pricing starts at Php 25,999 for the HONOR 600 5G (8GB+256GB) and goes up to Php 49,999 for the HONOR 600 5G Pro. To mark the launch, a promotion runs until May 29, 2026, offering pre-order customers the chance to win a Me

item.title: Photographs of Boston Streets During the Winter of 1960
item.description: American photographer Nick DeWolf documented Boston during the winter of 1960, producing a remarkable collection of images that reflect both the charm and the harshness of the season. His photographs depict iconic areas such as Beacon Hill, Back Bay, and Downtown Crossing, showing streets covered in thick snow, vintage cars navigating icy roads, and residents bundled in mid-century winter attire. The images capture not only the everyday life of Bostonians but also the architectural elegance of the city under a winter blanket, including the serene Public Garden and the historic brownstones of Back Bay. Snowbanks, icicles, and the glow of streetlights reflecting on frozen puddles highlight the season's quiet beauty. This visual time capsule offers a nostalgic glimpse into a resilient city, preserving its cultural and historical character during a classic New England winter. Overall, the collection serves as a tribute to Boston's timeless charm and the artistry of early street photography

item.title: Zsa Zsa Gabor’s Early Life and Career in the 1930s and 1940s
item.description: Zsa Zsa Gabor, born February 6, 1917, was a Hungarian-American socialite and actress, known for her glamorous presence and high-profile marriages. In the 1930s and 1940s, before she became a Hollywood film star, she was recognized for her beauty and European stage performances. Gabor was crowned Miss Hungary in 1936 but was later disqualified for being underage. She began her performing career after being discovered by operatic tenor Richard Tauber in Vienna in 1934, singing in the operetta 'The Singing Dream.' Her personal life drew public attention as well: in 1937, she married Burhan Asaf Belge and briefly lived in Ankara, claiming a short-lived romance with Mustafa Kemal Atatürk. She moved to the United States in 1941, following her sister Eva, and soon became a prominent figure in New York and Hollywood social circles. In 1942, she married hotel magnate Conrad Hilton, with whom she had her only child, Francesca Hilton, in 1947. This marriage was reportedly controlling, with Hilton

item.title: A Retrospective Look at Alice Cooper's Career and Iconic 1970s Portraits
item.description: Alice Cooper, born Vincent Damon Furnier on February 4, 1948, is an iconic American singer and songwriter renowned for his raspy voice and theatrical performances. Originally the name of a five-member band, Alice Cooper gained fame with hits like “I’m Eighteen” and the anthem “School’s Out.” Their 1973 album 'Billion Dollar Babies' topped charts in both the US and UK, and their tour shattered box-office records previously held by the Rolling Stones. The band’s live performances were legendary for their use of guillotines, electric chairs, fake blood, and live snakes. After the original group disbanded in 1975, Vince Furnier adopted the Alice Cooper name for his solo career. His 1975 concept album 'Welcome to My Nightmare' showcased his flair for theatricality, and he later explored softer ballads, including “Only Women Bleed” and “You and Me.” Cooper’s struggles with alcoholism inspired his 1978 album 'From the Inside.' Beyond music, he became a recognizable cultural figure with TV app

item.title: The 1950 Jaguar Mark V Drophead Coupe: Icon of British Post-War Automotive Design
item.description: The 1950 Jaguar Mark V Drophead Coupe represents one of the most refined examples of British luxury in the post-war era. Released at a critical time for Jaguar, the Mark V combined the elegance of pre-war automotive design with innovative engineering features. Notably, it was the first Jaguar model to implement independent front suspension and hydraulic brakes, marking a significant advancement in ride comfort and safety. The Drophead Coupe variant was especially celebrated for its versatile three-position soft top, allowing drivers to enjoy a fully closed, fully open, or semi-open 'de ville' driving experience. Beyond its engineering, the Mark V DHC impressed with its sweeping fenders, luxurious leather interior, and polished walnut dashboard, making it highly desirable among collectors today. The vehicle embodies the sophistication and understated elegance characteristic of early 1950s British motoring, standing as a testament to Jaguar’s ability to merge tradition with modernity.

item.title: Charity Event in 1982: Americans Smash Japanese Car to Support Laid-Off Steelworkers
item.description: In February 1982, a notable protest-cum-charity event took place in northern Indiana, where two American businessmen, Jim Coleman and Charlie Cobb, publicly destroyed a Japanese-made car. This act was intended to draw attention to the economic challenges faced by American auto and steel workers amid an increase in Japanese automobile imports. Organized with the support of local steelworkers’ union leaders, the event allowed attendees to pay one dollar per swing of a sledgehammer to strike the car. All proceeds from this unique fundraiser were directed toward supporting the families of workers who had been laid off due to economic downturns in the region. The scene, set against local businesses such as Radio Shack and Hoosier State Bank, reflected a mix of protest, community involvement, and a creative approach to charitable giving. While some might see the event as aggressive, it served as a symbolic outlet for community frustrations and a practical way to raise funds during a difficul

item.title: A Glimpse of Parisian Life in 1968 and 1970 Through Vintage Photos
item.description: This collection of vintage photographs provides an intimate look at daily life in Paris during the transformative years of 1968 and 1970. Captured shortly after the iconic May 1968 protests, the images reflect a city in flux—where the remnants of revolutionary spirit meet emerging fashion trends and the timeless charm of Parisian streets. The photos depict busy boulevards, iconic landmarks like the Eiffel Tower and Notre-Dame, quiet moments along the Seine, and people experiencing the evolving social and cultural landscape. From the bustling cafés to the serene gardens of Versailles and Luxembourg, each photograph preserves the atmosphere, energy, and character of Paris at the turn of a new decade, offering viewers a vivid glimpse into the lives of Parisians during a period of significant cultural change.

item.title: Robert Kearns’ Fight for Recognition Over Intermittent Windshield Wipers
item.description: In the 1960s, engineer Robert Kearns invented the intermittent windshield wiper, inspired by the human eye's blinking mechanism, which allows wipers to pause between swipes. After demonstrating his invention to Ford executives and sharing prototypes, he expected a licensing deal or partnership. Instead, Ford rejected his proposal but later released its own version of the intermittent wiper in 1969, which directly copied Kearns' patented design. Kearns filed a lawsuit against Ford in 1978, arguing that his invention represented a unique 'flash of genius,' despite using standard electronic components. The legal battle lasted over a decade, taking a severe personal toll on Kearns, including mental health struggles and the breakdown of his marriage. Representing himself during parts of the trial to uphold his principles, Kearns ultimately prevailed. In 1990, a jury confirmed that Ford had infringed on his patents, awarding him $5.2 million initially, which Ford later increased to $10.2 mil

item.title: American Doctor Under Observation in Prague After Ebola Exposure
item.description: An American doctor who was exposed to the Ebola virus has arrived in the Czech Republic and has been admitted to Prague's Bulovka University Hospital for preventive observation. Czech health authorities have implemented strict safety measures to ensure that the patient poses no risk to the public. This step is part of a broader international protocol for managing potential Ebola exposures, including immediate isolation, continuous health monitoring, and protective measures for hospital staff. The Czech Ministry of Health emphasized that the patient is currently stable and that all necessary precautions are in place to prevent any potential spread of the virus. Ebola, a severe viral disease with high mortality rates, has previously caused outbreaks primarily in Africa. This precautionary action demonstrates the importance of early intervention and monitoring, even in countries far from the primary outbreak zones. Authorities continue to stress that the risk to the general public remains

item.title: Congressional Amendment Proposes Nationwide Restriction on Police License Plate Tracking
item.description: U.S. lawmakers are set to introduce a bipartisan amendment that would prohibit recipients of federal highway funding from using automated license plate readers (ALPRs) for purposes other than toll collection. The amendment, proposed by Representative Scott Perry, a Republican, and Representative Jesús García, a Democrat, would effectively end state and local ALPR programs nationwide by conditioning federal funds on compliance. ALPR cameras, which are mounted on roads and police vehicles, capture license plate data, track vehicle movements, and store this information in searchable databases. Critics argue the technology poses significant privacy risks, including warrantless tracking, disproportionate targeting of certain communities, and misuse in sensitive cases, such as monitoring individuals seeking abortions. Past incidents have prompted legal challenges, including a class-action lawsuit in San Jose, California, alleging Fourth Amendment violations. While federal courts have yet to

item.title: Colossal Biosciences Develops 3D-Printed Artificial Eggshells to Grow Chicken Embryos
item.description: Colossal Biosciences has announced that it has successfully grown chicken embryos using 3D-printed artificial eggshells. The oval-shaped shells are created with a lattice structure coated internally with a silicone-based membrane that allows oxygen to pass through, mimicking the natural environment of a real eggshell. Researchers transfer the contents of freshly laid chicken eggs into these artificial shells, where the embryos continue to develop. The design includes a viewing window, enabling scientists to monitor the embryos in real-time. This technology could support the company’s broader goals of conserving endangered bird species and potentially resurrecting extinct birds, such as the dodo or the giant moa, although significant genetic engineering would be required for the latter. Currently, only chickens can be genetically modified through stem cell editing, which allows alterations to be passed to offspring. Colossal's research team is also exploring artificial wombs for mammals

item.title: The Life of Adelaide Springett: Childhood in East London's Spitalfields
item.description: In 1901, Horace Warner, a Quaker wallpaper designer and Sunday School superintendent, photographed eight-year-old Adelaide Springett, capturing a rare glimpse of childhood in the impoverished Spitalfields area of East London. Despite the photograph's title, “Adelaide Springett in All Her Best Clothes,” Adelaide’s bare feet, one wrapped in a cloth bandage, reveal the harsh realities of her upbringing. Her family struggled with extreme poverty; her parents worked as street sellers and casual dock laborers, and she endured multiple family tragedies, including the deaths of her sisters and her mother’s early death. Records indicate that she lived in a Salvation Army shelter and previously in the notorious Miller’s Court, infamous for Jack the Ripper’s crimes. Adelaide went on to work in domestic service, survived both World Wars, and lived to the age of 93, passing away in 1986 without immediate family. Warner’s daughter recalled that the portrait, nicknamed “Little Adelaide’s best and onl

item.title: 1930s Street Fashion: Elegance and Style During the Great Depression
item.description: Street fashion in the 1930s represented a shift toward elegance and sophistication, moving away from the playful flapper silhouettes of the 1920s. Despite the economic challenges of the Great Depression, both men and women placed significant emphasis on style. Women's fashion focused on the natural waistline, with hemlines returning to mid-calf or ankle length. Daywear often included floral prints, puffed sleeves, and the innovative 'bias cut,' which allowed garments to drape gracefully over the body. For men, the 'Superman' silhouette became iconic, featuring wide-shouldered double-breasted suits, high-waisted trousers, and the essential fedora. Accessories such as hats, gloves, and furs allowed everyday individuals to emulate the glamour of Hollywood stars. The era showcased a clever balance between practicality and sophistication, reflecting resilience and creativity during difficult times. Vintage photographs from this period capture the refined street style and cultural atmosphere

item.title: Florida International University bans GOP students over racist and violent group chat messages
item.description: Florida International University has banned two student members of the College Republicans after their involvement in a scandal involving racist and violent group chat messages. Abel Carvajal, a third-year law student and the creator of the chat, was found responsible for actions violating the university's Student Code of Conduct. He is appealing the decision. Dariel Gonzalez, the former recruitment chairman for FIU College Republicans, was also sanctioned for verbal abuse, threats, and actions endangering the safety of others, as well as driving under the influence and using marijuana on campus. The group chat, which surfaced over two months ago, contained graphic fantasies of violence and racial slurs, including plans to harm Black people. This incident echoes a prior scandal involving national Young Republicans members who made similarly offensive comments and fantasies. Legal representation for Gonzalez is being provided by Anthony Sabatini, a former far-right lawmaker. The univers

item.title: 19th-Century Photographs Documenting the Sámi People by Roche and Bonaparte
item.description: In 1884, Prince Roland Bonaparte organized an anthropological expedition to Northern Norway (Finnmark) and Sweden to document the Sámi people. Most of the photographs were taken by G. Roche, the expedition’s photographer. Unlike modern photographers who often capture candid moments, Roche and Bonaparte used formal 'en face' and profile shots, a quasi-scientific approach aimed at recording physical features. The collection features over 100 portraits, with many subjects identified by name, including Jol Andersen, Anna Hurri, and Niels Larsen Pilto. Subjects are often dressed in traditional Sámi clothing, called gákti, and posed against neutral backdrops or within rigid frames. Some portraits even include measurement tools or number plates, reflecting the era’s interest in racial classification. Beyond portraits, the expedition documented Sámi dwellings (lavvu), boats, and the Arctic environment, providing a broader cultural and historical context of life in Northern Norway during the 18

item.title: Remembering Jane Birkin: Icon of 1960s–70s Fashion and Film
item.description: Jane Birkin (1946–2023) was an emblematic figure whose unique blend of British cool and French elegance defined the style of the 1960s and 1970s. Born in London and later embraced by Paris, she gained worldwide recognition through her creative and personal partnership with Serge Gainsbourg, especially with the controversial duet that captured the era’s cultural imagination. Beyond music, Birkin’s film appearances in classics like Blow-Up and La Piscine contributed to her status as a cultural icon. Her influence on fashion remains profound, exemplified by the Hermès Birkin bag, which was inspired by a chance encounter on an airplane and has since become a symbol of luxury. Known for her distinctive bangs, casual bohemian style, and natural elegance, Birkin’s aesthetic continues to inspire designers and fashion enthusiasts around the globe. Vintage photos from the 1960s and 1970s showcase her effortless charm and enduring style, cementing her legacy as a muse whose impact transcends both

item.title: Trump Administration Establishes Fund for Allies Following IRS Lawsuit Settlement, Raising Legal and Ethical Questions
item.description: On May 20, 2026, the U.S. Department of Justice announced the creation of a $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund,' intended to compensate individuals who claim they were targeted for political prosecution, including potential claims against the Biden administration. The fund was established as part of a settlement in which President Donald Trump dropped his $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS, which alleged harm from leaked tax returns. Critics argue the fund could benefit Trump allies, including participants in the January 6, 2021, Capitol attack, raising concerns about self-dealing and conflicts of interest. Legal scholars highlight that allowing a president to effectively settle a lawsuit in which he is a party may violate longstanding moral and legal principles, including the maxim 'no one should be a judge in their own cause.' Questions also arise about constitutional authority, separation of powers, and potential violations of the Emoluments Clause and Section 4 of the 14th Am

item.title: Smaller and Less Prestigious Roles Can Provide Valuable Career Experience
item.description: This editorial argues that overemphasizing brand names and elite institutions diminishes the value of genuine skills and responsibility. Students often pursue prestigious internships and roles, focusing on the name rather than the substance of their work, resulting in limited engagement and what many describe as 'busy work.' Elite institutions and companies favor candidates from a narrow set of prestigious universities, prioritizing cultural fit, social grooming, and extracurricular prestige over actual skills. Meanwhile, smaller or less glamorous roles—such as managing a local nonprofit, working at a community newspaper, or helping run a family-owned business—provide authentic experience, direct responsibility, and opportunities for meaningful growth. Research indicates that task variety, feedback, and real interaction with professionals significantly enhance students' self-efficacy and career development, outcomes often easier to achieve in nonelite settings. The editorial also empha

item.title: Street Life in Vienna in the Mid-1980s Through Vintage Photography
item.description: The mid-1980s in Vienna were a unique period where the city appeared suspended between its grand imperial history and a modern world yet to fully embrace digital technology. Vintage photographs shared by Morton1905 reveal the distinctive charm of the era. Red-and-white trams moved rhythmically along the Ringstraße, passing historic facades that carried the patina of age. In the Innere Stadt, narrow cobblestone alleys were filled with the aromas of roasting coffee from traditional Konditoreis and the faint trace of winter coal smoke. Residents dressed in classic loden coats and wide-brimmed hats navigated the streets, while younger generations added vibrant energy with leather jackets and bold New Wave fashion. Public squares offered spaces of relaxed social interaction, embodying the Austrian concept of “Gemütlichkeit,” a cozy and welcoming atmosphere. The photos capture both everyday routines and fleeting cultural expressions, from smoke-filled coffee houses to the elegance of Vienna’

item.title: Frank Zappa's Quick-Witted Comeback to Joe Pyne in 1966 Talk Show
item.description: In 1966, legendary musician Frank Zappa appeared on The Joe Pyne Show, a talk show famous for its confrontational style and aggressive host, Joe Pyne. Known for his provocative and sometimes insulting approach, Pyne immediately criticized Zappa's long hair, implying that it made him appear effeminate. Zappa, however, responded with remarkable composure and wit, turning the insult back on Pyne by referencing Pyne's own wooden leg, saying, 'Well, Joe, you have a wooden leg. I suppose your wooden leg makes you a table.' This sharp retort became one of the most iconic moments in television history, illustrating both the cultural tensions of the 1960s and the power of humor over hostility. Though no full recording of the interview survives today, the anecdote continues to be celebrated and cited as an example of Zappa's cleverness and ability to navigate public confrontation with intelligence and style. The exchange reflects broader themes of counterculture versus mainstream norms during a

item.title: Sociological and Economic Factors Reducing the Likelihood of War Over Taiwan
item.description: The article examines the potential for a global conflict over Taiwan, emphasizing the importance of sociological and economic realities in shaping international relations. It analyzes Donald Trump's visit to China and his meeting with President Xi Jinping, noting that while political concerns such as Taiwan's defense, trade, and military sales remain unresolved, both leaders appear to prioritize economic stability over military confrontation. The author highlights the diminishing relevance of the 'Thucydides Trap'—the idea that rising powers inevitably clash with established ones—citing China's slowing economy, mounting debts, demographic challenges, and technological shifts that reduce the country's readiness for war. Similarly, economic pressures and demographic trends in other nations dampen enthusiasm for military conflict. The article suggests that effective contraception, changing workforce demographics, and financial interdependence have become key factors discouraging war, fram

item.title: Ebola Outbreak in Central Africa Worsens Amid US Aid Cuts
item.description: The ongoing Ebola outbreak in Central and East Africa is being exacerbated by significant reductions in US foreign aid and global health funding. Public health workers report severe shortages of essential medical supplies such as masks, hand sanitizers, and testing equipment due to funding cuts. The closure of the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) and reductions in CDC support have fragmented the region's disease response infrastructure, leaving healthcare personnel overworked and stretched thin. The World Health Organization declared the outbreak a public health emergency of international concern on May 16, 2026. The Bundibugyo strain of Ebola, which currently has no vaccine or treatment, has already resulted in over 530 confirmed cases and 134 deaths, with mortality rates estimated between 25% and 50%. Experts warn that delayed responses and inadequate resources have made containment more challenging and may allow the virus to spread beyond Africa, including

item.title: Axl Rose Poses with Early Mobile Phone at Sunset Grill in 1988
item.description: In 1988, iconic Guns N’ Roses frontman Axl Rose was photographed by renowned rock photographer Mark Weiss at the Sunset Grill in Hollywood. The photos, taken during a brief off-tour period in the spring, capture Rose in a natural and candid moment as he enjoyed a quick meal with his then-girlfriend, Erin Everly. The session was originally intended to include the whole band, but only Axl showed up the following day. During the shoot, Rose showcased a handheld mobile phone, marking Weiss’s first encounter with such a device. Weiss emphasized that the session was informal and not staged, reflecting a rare, unposed glimpse of the rock star. The photographs highlight Rose’s casual appearance, including a beard, which was uncommon in many other images of the singer from that period. Mark Weiss, also known as “Weissguy,” is celebrated for capturing defining images of 1980s rock and glam metal icons such as Ozzy Osbourne, Bon Jovi, Metallica, and Guns N’ Roses. These photographs provide both a

item.title: Merna Kennedy: Remembering the Silent Film Actress of the 1920s
item.description: Merna Kennedy (1908–1944) was a talented American actress known for her contributions to the film industry during the transition from silent films to sound films. She gained fame through her performance in Charlie Chaplin's 1928 film, The Circus, where she played a circus rider and Chaplin's love interest. Kennedy's introduction to Chaplin came through her childhood friend Lita Grey, who later became Chaplin’s second wife. Kennedy's natural charm, expressive facial acting, and stage presence allowed her to shine during a time when silent films demanded highly visual storytelling. Following The Circus, she appeared in several other films, including Broadway (1929) and Lummox (1930). Despite her early success, Kennedy’s career struggled as the film industry evolved, and she retired from acting after marrying the renowned choreographer Busby Berkeley. Tragically, she passed away from a heart attack at just 35 years old. Today, she is remembered as an emblematic figure of 1920s Hollywood g

item.title: Bob Marley’s Life and Career in the 1970s: From the Wailers to Global Icon
item.description: Bob Marley, born on February 6, 1945, was a Jamaican singer, songwriter, and guitarist who became one of the most influential figures in reggae music. In the 1970s, Marley’s career transformed dramatically. He evolved from performing with the original Wailers trio alongside Peter Tosh and Bunny Wailer to leading Bob Marley & The Wailers, with the backing of the I-Threes and the Barrett brothers. The signing with Island Records in 1972 led to the international release of albums like Catch a Fire and Burnin’, which helped introduce reggae to audiences worldwide. Marley’s global breakthrough came in 1975 with the live version of 'No Woman, No Cry,' followed by the critically acclaimed album Exodus in 1977, later named the best album of the 20th century by Time Magazine. Despite his rising fame, Jamaica’s political violence deeply affected him. In December 1976, Marley survived a politically motivated shooting and later moved to London for two years, producing some of his most iconic work,

item.title: Giants Running Back Cam Skattebo Projects Unrealistic 2,000-Yard Season
item.description: New York Giants running back Cam Skattebo, coming off a promising rookie season in 2025, sparked discussion after projecting a 2,000-yard rushing season for 2026. Skattebo recorded 400 yards on 100 carries as a rookie before suffering a significant ankle injury that ended his season prematurely. Known for his toughness and physical running style, he has quickly become a fan favorite and a key asset for the Giants. However, during a recent interview, Skattebo confidently claimed that with 300 carries, he would surpass 2,000 yards in the upcoming season. Simple calculations show that maintaining his rookie average would yield closer to 1,200 yards over a full season—still a strong performance but far from the lofty 2,000-yard figure he projected. Analysts and fans alike noted that while his optimism is admirable, the numbers don't quite add up. Despite the questionable projection, the Giants value Skattebo for his on-field skills, leadership, and resilience rather than his mathematical a

item.title: White House Signals GOP Should Accept Trump's Leadership Amid Party Divisions
item.description: As the Republican Party experiences growing internal divisions, a senior White House official has urged GOP members to recognize and accept former President Donald Trump's influence over the party. The official emphasized that resisting Trump's dominance is a futile effort, given his continued sway over Republican voters. Trump's endorsements have successfully led to the defeat of GOP lawmakers who opposed him in recent primary elections in Kentucky, Louisiana, and Indiana. Despite pushback from some Republicans, including threats against Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick for voting against Trump, the White House continues to highlight Trump's role as the leading figure and motivator for the party. House Majority Leader Steve Scalise also reinforced the notion that voter support ultimately determines political survival, indirectly referencing GOP Senator Bill Cassidy's primary loss in Louisiana. The situation underscores ongoing tensions within the Republican Party between loyalty to Trump and in

item.title: CNN panel debates Jeff Bezos's proposal to exempt low-income workers from income tax
item.description: On May 20, 2026, a CNN 'NewsNight' panel debated Amazon founder Jeff Bezos's new tax proposal, which he revealed in an interview with Aaron Ross Sorkin on CNBC. Bezos suggested that the lowest-income workers should not pay income tax, arguing that this approach would benefit middle-income earners, such as nurses in Queens, more than plans proposed by Democrats like Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani. During the discussion, attorney Arthur Aidala defended Bezos's position, claiming higher taxes on the wealthy could harm the middle class by prompting wealthy individuals to move to lower-tax states. However, journalist Charles Blow and other panelists challenged Aidala, asking for data to support these claims. Blow highlighted that millionaires continue moving to New York despite Mamdani's policies, disputing the notion that raising taxes drives the wealthy away. The debate grew heated as Aidala could not provide concrete evidence, and panelists

item.title: Psychiatrist Warns of Potential Risk in Trump's Decision-Making During Second Term
item.description: Dr. John Gartner, a psychiatrist and former Johns Hopkins University professor, expressed serious concerns about President Donald Trump's mental state and decision-making capabilities in a recent podcast interview. Speaking with Hugh Dougherty on 'The Daily Beast Podcast,' Gartner suggested that Trump's recent political events, such as Rep. Thomas Massie's primary defeat, could embolden the president to take drastic and unprecedented actions. Specifically, Gartner warned that Trump might consider launching a nuclear strike, potentially without anyone in his administration to prevent it. Gartner believes Trump exhibits signs of frontotemporal dementia, a condition that can impair judgment, inhibition, and decision-making. He argues that the president's behavior shows a lack of restraint and that his current cabinet consists of loyalists who would not act as a check on his impulses. Gartner noted that previous safeguards, like experienced military and political advisors, are no longer in

item.title: Dana White Reports Insect Infestation in Trump’s Renovated Rose Garden
item.description: Dana White, the CEO of the Ultimate Fighting Championship and longtime supporter of former President Donald Trump, revealed an extensive insect problem in the newly renovated Rose Garden at the White House. White described seeing swarms of gnats during a dinner at the garden, which he said was so significant that it could potentially interfere with a UFC Fight Night planned on the White House lawn in June to celebrate Trump's 80th birthday and America's 250th anniversary. To mitigate the issue, White suggested installing fans around the venue, noting that gnats struggle against wind. The Rose Garden had been completely overhauled last year, with grass removed and a more Mar-a-Lago-style aesthetic implemented. Trump has also made other controversial modifications to the White House, including gold decorations and the demolition of the historic East Wing to build a ballroom. Despite the insect issue, Trump remains optimistic about the event, expecting thousands of spectators for the UFC

item.title: Jacqueline Bisset's Iconic Role and Style in 'Bullitt' (1968)
item.description: In the 1968 film 'Bullitt,' Jacqueline Bisset delivers a memorable performance as Cathy, the girlfriend of Steve McQueen’s character, the tough detective Frank Bullitt. Though her role is secondary, Bisset's presence brings warmth and humanity to the film, offering a contrast to its intense action scenes. The portraits taken on set highlight her ethereal beauty and sophisticated 1960s fashion sense, capturing moments of elegance, vulnerability, and timeless glamour. These images provide a visual balance to the film’s gritty San Francisco backdrop, illustrating the peaceful life Bullitt strives to protect. Bisset’s portrayal, alongside the carefully crafted cinematography, contributes to her enduring status as a classic Hollywood star. Her style and poise in these behind-the-scenes portraits have become iconic representations of 1960s cinematic elegance, showcasing both her charm and the era’s cultural aesthetic. The photos not only celebrate her acting career but also offer a glimpse i

item.title: Astronomers Discover Unexpected Magnetic Reversal in the Milky Way's Sagittarius Arm
item.description: Researchers at the University of Calgary have uncovered a previously hidden magnetic structure within the Milky Way, revealing a diagonal magnetic field reversal in the Sagittarius Arm. Utilizing the Dominion Radio Astrophysical Observatory’s new radio telescope, part of the Global Magneto-Ionic Medium Survey (GMIMS), the team produced one of the most detailed maps of the galaxy's magnetic field to date. By measuring Faraday rotation, an effect in which radio waves are altered by electrons and magnetic fields, scientists were able to trace complex magnetic patterns across vast distances. The discovery of a counterclockwise magnetic region within an otherwise clockwise field challenges existing models of galactic structure and could reshape our understanding of how magnetic fields influence the stability and evolution of the Milky Way. Lead researchers Dr. Jo-Anne Brown, Dr. Anna Ordog, and PhD candidate Rebecca Booth published their findings in The Astrophysical Journal and its Supplem

item.title: Grace Kelly’s 1956 Wedding Dress: Design, Legacy, and Influence
item.description: Grace Kelly’s wedding dress, worn during her marriage to Prince Rainier III of Monaco on April 19, 1956, remains one of the most iconic bridal gowns of the 20th century. Designed by Helen Rose of MGM, the dress featured a high-necked, long-sleeved fitted bodice and a full skirt, inspired by costumes in the MGM archives, particularly from the film Invitation. Made with luxurious materials including silk taffeta, silk net, peau de soie, tulle, and 125-year-old Brussels rose point lace, the ensemble also included a bejeweled Juliet cap, a 90-yard tulle veil, kid gloves, and a lace- and pearl-encrusted prayer book. The civil ceremony the day before was attended by 80 guests from 24 nations, and Kelly wore a pale pink taffeta dress covered with cream-colored Alençon lace. The wedding gown has influenced many modern designs, including those of Catherine Middleton, Australian model Miranda Kerr, and Paris Hilton. The Philadelphia Museum of Art displayed the dress on its 50th anniversary, call

item.title: ATRenew Inc. Reports Strong Q1 2026 Earnings, Revenue Up 40% Year-Over-Year
item.description: ATRenew Inc. (RERE) announced its first-quarter results for 2026, exceeding analyst expectations on both earnings per share and revenue. The company reported a Q1 EPS of $0.09, surpassing estimates by $0.02. Revenue reached $903.98 million, representing a 40.25% year-over-year increase and beating projections by $44.16 million. These results reflect strong operational performance and continued growth momentum for ATRenew. The earnings call presentation, released on May 20, 2026, provided detailed insights into the company's financials, highlighting key drivers behind the revenue growth and profitability. Seeking Alpha's transcripts team compiled and published the related slide deck to provide investors with comprehensive coverage of the quarterly earnings discussion. The release emphasizes transparency and aims to inform shareholders about ATRenew's strategic initiatives and financial health. Overall, the company's positive performance underscores its robust market position and the eff

item.title: Anne Sullivan and Helen Keller: A Lifelong Story of Education and Perseverance
item.description: The friendship between Anne Sullivan and Helen Keller stands as a remarkable example of human resilience and the transformative power of education. In 1882, Helen Keller, at just nineteen months old, lost her sight and hearing due to an illness, likely scarlet fever or meningitis. By age six, she struggled with communication, often resorting to frustration and violent behavior. Her parents sought help from the Perkins Institution for the Blind, which sent Anne Sullivan, a young teacher who had partially regained her own sight after multiple surgeries. Sullivan began teaching Keller by spelling words into her hand using the manual alphabet, though Keller initially did not understand the connection between the words and objects. The breakthrough came on April 5, 1887, when Sullivan demonstrated the word 'water' at an outdoor pump, awakening Keller’s understanding that objects have names. This moment marked the start of Keller's incredible intellectual journey, as she quickly learned new

item.title: Trump DOJ indictment of Raúl Castro signals broader strategy toward Cuba
item.description: The Trump administration's Department of Justice recently indicted Cuban leader Raúl Castro for a 1996 airplane shootdown that killed four men, including three Americans. Legal expert Melba Pearson, a former South Florida prosecutor, told the Legal AF podcast that this indictment is unlikely to result in Castro’s extradition or a trial due to the lack of a U.S.-Cuba extradition agreement. Despite this, the move signals a broader political and strategic aim. According to Pearson, the indictment restricts Castro's ability to travel or access international assets, suggesting a potential preparation for U.S. intervention or influence in Cuba. The timing also aligns with political considerations, as Cuban-American voters in Florida are a significant voting bloc that supported President Trump strongly. The administration has reportedly been threatening military action against Cuba for months, with reports that the Cuban government is preparing by purchasing drones. Pearson interprets the ind

item.title: SpaceX IPO Filing Highlights AI Investments, Starship Development, and Elon Musk's Leadership
item.description: SpaceX, founded by Elon Musk 24 years ago, has publicly filed for its IPO, setting the stage for one of the largest offerings in history. The S-1 filing reveals a diversified technology company that extends far beyond its original goal of reusable rockets, with significant investments in satellite internet (Starlink) and artificial intelligence through XAI. In 2025, SpaceX reported $18 billion in revenue but a $4.9 billion loss, reflecting the company's $37 billion cumulative losses since inception. Starlink remains the core revenue driver, generating more than half of last year’s income. The company plans to rely on its fully reusable Starship rocket for satellite launches and future projects like Mars exploration and orbital AI data centers. SpaceX aims to reduce orbital launch costs by up to 99% and sees Starship as pivotal to its long-term growth. The filing also outlines futuristic plans, including point-to-point Earth transport, space tourism, in-orbit and lunar manufacturing, an

item.title: The Life and Legacy of Lee Remick: Hollywood’s Elegant Star
item.description: Lee Remick (1935–1991) was a celebrated American actress renowned for her intelligence, poise, and versatility on screen. Emerging as a notable talent in the late 1950s, she received critical acclaim for her performances in films such as 'Anatomy of a Murder' (1959), alongside James Stewart, and 'Days of Wine and Roses' (1962), which earned her an Academy Award nomination. Remick was admired not only for her refined beauty but also for her ability to portray deeply complex and emotionally nuanced characters, excelling in both drama and thriller genres, with roles like in 'The Omen' (1976) standing out. Her career extended beyond cinema to include stage and television, where she consistently brought sophistication and depth to every role. Vintage photographs of a young Lee Remick capture the elegance, intelligence, and timeless charm that defined her presence both on and off the screen. Remembered as one of Hollywood’s most respected mid-20th-century actresses, Remick’s contributions co

item.title: Remembering Françoise Dorléac: Icon of 1960s French Cinema
item.description: Françoise Dorléac was one of the brightest stars of 1960s French cinema, known for her charisma, elegance, and vivacious energy. Rising to fame during the French New Wave, she delivered memorable performances in films like 'That Man from Rio' and François Truffaut’s 'The Soft Skin.' Her role in the 1967 musical 'The Young Girls of Rochefort,' alongside her sister Catherine Deneuve, highlighted the contrast between Dorléac’s lively brunette persona and Deneuve’s composed blonde image. Despite her career being tragically cut short at the age of 25, Dorléac left an enduring mark on French film culture and is remembered as the embodiment of 1960s sophistication and artistic vitality. Her contributions continue to inspire filmmakers and actors, and her performances are celebrated for their depth, charm, and natural grace, ensuring that her legacy remains an integral part of cinematic history.

item.title: Rep. Nancy Mace faces criticism for proposing bill restricting office to natural-born citizens
item.description: Rep. Nancy Mace (R-SC) has proposed legislation that would bar naturalized citizens from holding any elected office in the United States, a move that has drawn criticism from both Democrats and some Republicans. Mace posted on X, stating that anyone not born in the U.S. should not hold government power. Currently, naturalized citizens are constitutionally barred only from the presidency, but they may hold other offices if they meet certain residency and citizenship requirements. Democratic Rep. Delia Ramirez pointed out that Mace's proposal could also affect Republican colleagues who were not born in the U.S., such as Florida Rep. Carlos Giménez. Critics have labeled Mace's bill as xenophobic and potentially divisive, noting that her approach risks alienating voters ahead of her campaign for governor of South Carolina. This legislation has sparked a broader debate about eligibility and fairness for naturalized citizens in American politics, highlighting tensions within both parties as

item.title: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche comments on payout eligibility for Jan. 6 convicts
item.description: During a CNN interview on May 20, 2026, Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche made remarks that drew widespread attention and criticism. When asked by journalist Paula Reid whether individuals convicted of harming police officers during the January 6, 2021 insurrection could receive funds from a new $1.776 billion Department of Justice settlement tied to former President Donald Trump, Blanche stated that people who hurt police receive compensation through legal channels if they believe their rights were violated. This statement has sparked concern and debate over the administration's stance on accountability and law enforcement. Critics highlighted the potential implications of the settlement, which also includes provisions preventing the IRS from auditing Trump, his family, or affiliated entities. Social media reactions underscored public shock and disapproval, framing Blanche's comments as emblematic of a pro-crime approach within the administration. Analysts noted the contrast betwee

item.title: Victorian Studio Portraits Document a Variety of 19th-Century Occupations
item.description: In the 19th century, photography studios became a unique venue for the working class to preserve their professional identities. Unlike candid street photos, these studio portraits presented individuals with the specific tools and attire of their trades, reflecting pride and dignity in their work. The collection captures a wide array of occupations, including chimney sweeps, fishmongers, maids, farriers, cobblers, and photographers, among many others. Each portrait offers a detailed look at uniforms, equipment, and the societal roles of the era, acting as a visual record of vanished crafts and the hierarchical structure of Victorian society. These images not only provide historical insight but also celebrate the personal and professional pride of ordinary individuals who contributed to the functioning of the Victorian world. By carefully posing with their tools, these workers transformed a simple photograph into a testament of their trade and legacy, preserving a tangible connection to

item.title: Matthew Perry and Jennifer Aniston Star in 1995 Windows 95 Instructional Video
item.description: In 1995, during the height of their fame on the TV show Friends, Matthew Perry and Jennifer Aniston appeared in a unique promotional video for Microsoft titled the 'Windows 95 Video Guide.' Marketed as the 'world's first cyber sitcom,' the 60-minute VHS production aimed to teach users the essentials of the new Windows 95 operating system. In the video, Perry and Aniston play themselves, visiting Bill Gates' office to audition for a Microsoft video guide. They are introduced to the top 25 features of Windows 95, including the Start menu, right-click functions, multitasking, and the Recycle Bin, all explained through interactions with quirky characters such as a martial arts guru and 'Joystick Johnny.' The video also captures cultural moments of the mid-1990s, like Aniston calling copy-pasting 'trippy' and Perry figuring out how to email a friend in a grunge band. The VHS is organized into three sections: the cyber sitcom, a detailed technical walkthrough, and answers to 20 frequently as

item.title: Agriculture Secretary Attributes Rising Beef Prices to Increased Demand and Supply Challenges
item.description: Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins attributed recent spikes in beef prices to a combination of factors, including increased consumer demand linked to the Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) movement led by Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. The movement has promoted red meat, organ meats, and animal fats as part of a health-focused diet, encouraging Americans to consume more beef. Ground beef prices have reached approximately $6.70 to $6.90 per pound, roughly a dollar above historical averages, as U.S. cattle herds remain at a three-generation low. Rollins also pointed to regulatory pressures under the Biden administration, including reduced grazing allotments and climate-related policies, as contributing to the strain on domestic beef production. Supply chain disruptions, such as a screwworm infestation in South America affecting ports, have further limited availability. Rollins highlighted ongoing Department of Justice scrutiny of major beef processors, some fo

item.title: Unpatched Chromium Vulnerability Exposed by Google Could Affect Millions of Browsers
item.description: Google recently published proof-of-concept exploit code for a critical, yet unpatched, vulnerability in its Chromium browser codebase. This security flaw impacts not only Google Chrome but also Microsoft Edge and nearly all other Chromium-based browsers. The vulnerability abuses the Browser Fetch API, which allows large files and long videos to download in the background. Exploit code can create persistent connections, monitor users' browser activity, enable anonymous proxy access, and facilitate denial-of-service attacks. In some browsers, these connections persist even after a reboot, effectively creating a limited botnet from affected devices. The flaw was discovered by independent researcher Lyra Rebane in late 2022 and reported privately to Google. Despite a 29-month delay, the vulnerability remained unpatched when Google published the exploit code on the Chromium bug tracker, though it was later removed. However, the code remains accessible through archival sites. Security expert

item.title: Senate Republicans Consider Restrictions on Trump’s Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description: Senate Republicans are considering measures to impose restrictions on how former President Donald Trump can distribute funds from his $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund.' The fund, established as a settlement after Trump dropped a $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS, aims to provide financial support to pro-Trump individuals allegedly targeted by legal investigations, potentially including participants in the January 6 Capitol attack. Republicans are exploring ways to set conditions for eligibility to prevent controversial payouts, especially to individuals who engaged in violent actions. Senate Majority Leader John Thune has expressed skepticism about the fund's purpose, and even Republican lawmakers are requesting more details from the Justice Department about its administration. This debate may become part of a broader reconciliation package focused on immigration enforcement, with potential amendments introduced during a 'vote-a-rama' to address Democratic concerns. Democrats

item.title: James Dean’s Teenage Years in 1940s Indiana
item.description: Before becoming the iconic 'Rebel Without a Cause,' James Dean was a typical teenager growing up in Fairmount, Indiana, during the 1940s. At Fairmount High School, he excelled in sports, particularly basketball, baseball, and pole vaulting, even setting a county record in pole vault. His artistic side also emerged early, encouraged by his drama teacher Adeline Nall. Dean participated in numerous school plays, public speaking competitions, and forensics events, while also playing drums and bongos. Known for his adventurous streak, he received a motorized bicycle for graduation, which he later traded for a motorcycle, performing daring stunts around town. Peers described him as both popular and introspective, often enjoying time on his aunt and uncle's farm or lost in daydreams. Graduating in May 1949, Dean moved back to California that summer, leaving behind his small-town upbringing and stepping toward his path in Hollywood.

item.title: Jet2 Expands Summer 2026 Schedule with 37 New Routes and 3 New Destinations
item.description: Jet2 has announced a significant expansion of its summer 2026 operations, introducing 37 new routes and three new destinations—La Palma, Palermo, and Samos—bringing the airline's total overseas destinations to 66. This expansion increases Jet2's overall summer network to 532 services. The Leeds-based airline is focusing on Mediterranean markets, particularly Greece, Spain, Portugal, and the Balearic Islands, with Greece seeing the largest seat increase at 446,000. Gatwick Airport is the main driver of growth this summer, marking Jet2's 14th UK base with six aircraft, including five Airbus A321neos. The airport alone contributes 908,000 additional seats. Other regional UK bases, including Bournemouth, Liverpool, Birmingham, Manchester, Edinburgh, Glasgow, London Stansted, East Midlands, Newcastle, Leeds Bradford, and Belfast International, also see network expansions. Looking ahead, Jet2 plans to enter the Egyptian market in February 2027, adding Hurghada and Sharm El Sheikh, and antici

item.title: Universities increasingly rescind commencement invitations over controversial views
item.description: In recent years, universities across the United States have increasingly rescinded invitations to commencement speakers due to student protests over controversial viewpoints. Former Northwestern University president Morton Schapiro withdrew from Georgetown University Law Center’s graduation after students opposed his planned speech, citing his past pro-Israel statements. Free speech advocates describe this pattern as a form of 'heckler’s veto,' where anticipated audience reactions prevent speakers from participating. Historically, U.S. commencement addresses began in 1642 without outside speakers, evolving by the 19th century to include notable figures like Ralph Waldo Emerson and U.S. presidents such as James Garfield and John F. Kennedy, who faced little to no controversy. Today, however, disinvitations occur at both large public universities and small liberal arts colleges, prompted by petitions or public outcry over political, religious, or social positions. Notable cases include B

item.title: D.C. Schools Chancellor Lewis Ferebee Resigns to Lead Education Nonprofit
item.description: Lewis D. Ferebee, the longest-serving schools chancellor in Washington, D.C., announced his departure from the role to become the CEO of EdReports, a national education nonprofit. Appointed in 2018, Ferebee played a central role in guiding the district through the aftermath of the COVID-19 pandemic, leading efforts that helped improve both math and reading scores across the city. During his tenure, Ferebee focused on implementing strategies to support student learning recovery, addressing educational gaps exacerbated by pandemic-related school closures. His leadership was marked by a commitment to academic progress and equity within D.C. public schools. The move to EdReports represents a shift from local administration to a broader national focus on educational evaluation and policy, reflecting Ferebee’s ongoing dedication to improving learning outcomes. The announcement comes as the district seeks a new leader to continue building on the progress made in recent years, with emphasis on

item.title: Vanessa Trump Announces Breast Cancer Diagnosis and Shares Update on Treatment
item.description: Vanessa Trump, formerly married to Donald Trump Jr., publicly disclosed that she has been diagnosed with breast cancer. In an Instagram post, she stated that she is working closely with her medical team to establish a treatment plan. Vanessa, 48, was married to Trump Jr. for 12 years, with whom she shares five children, before their divorce in 2018. Currently, she is in a relationship with professional golfer Tiger Woods. The announcement has prompted supportive messages from family members, including Ivanka Trump, who commented on Vanessa's post expressing prayers for her strength and a swift recovery. The revelation has attracted media attention, highlighting both Vanessa's personal health journey and her connections to prominent figures in American public life. Vanessa emphasized that while the diagnosis was unexpected, she remains proactive about managing her health and focusing on treatment.

item.title: Conservative commentator criticizes Alex Cooper’s lifestyle following her pregnancy announcement
item.description: Alex Cooper, host of the popular podcast 'Call Her Daddy,' recently announced her pregnancy with husband Matt Kaplan, sparking criticism from conservative commentator Allie Beth Stuckey of the 'Relatable' podcast on BlazeTV. Stuckey criticized Cooper for what she perceives as abandoning the liberal, independent lifestyle she previously promoted to her largely female audience. Stuckey described Cooper's podcast as primarily focused on sexual gossip and labeled her a 'podcast prostitute,' arguing that Cooper encourages women to embrace liberal values and promiscuity over traditional gender roles. The commentary also touched on broader political and cultural concerns, with Stuckey claiming that mainstream media tolerates extreme left-leaning voices while marginalizing conservative or Christian perspectives. The criticism comes amidst wider debates in U.S. politics, including declining birth rates and discussions on media representation of conservative versus liberal figures. Cooper’s pers

item.title: Fox News Poll Shows Rising Economic Concerns and Declining Trump Approval
item.description:
A recent Fox News national survey highlights growing economic concerns among American voters and declining approval ratings for former President Donald Trump. The poll shows that 58% of respondents identify the cost of living as their top economic worry, up from 50% in February, surpassing other concerns like government spending, jobs, and tariffs. Overall, 77% of voters rate the economy poorly, marking a steady increase from previous months. Personal finances are also under pressure, with 51% saying their family’s finances have worsened over the past two years.
Trump’s approval on handling the economy has declined to 29%, down from 34% in April. Disapproval of his performance has reached 71%, including growing disapproval among Republicans and non-MAGA Republicans. Inflation ratings are particularly low, with only 24% approving of his handling, and border security ratings have slipped to a near-even split among voters.
The survey also finds that gas prices are a major concern, with

item.title: Early Career of Lana Turner: Portraits from the 1930s
item.description: Lana Turner, born Julia Jean Turner on February 8, 1921, rose from modest beginnings to become one of Hollywood's most iconic actresses. In the mid-1930s, she was a student at Hollywood High School when she was discovered by William R. Wilkerson, publisher of The Hollywood Reporter. This chance encounter led to a screen test and her signing by director Mervyn LeRoy, who suggested she adopt the stage name Lana Turner. She made her debut in the 1937 film 'They Won’t Forget,' where her appearance in a fitted sweater earned her the nickname 'The Sweater Girl,' a label she disliked because it emphasized her physical appearance over her talent. Originally contracted by Warner Bros. for $50 a week, Turner followed LeRoy to MGM in 1938, where her career accelerated. Her transformation from brunette to blonde for the 1939 film 'Idiot’s Delight' became her signature look, earning her media attention as the 'Nightclub Queen.' By the end of the 1930s, Turner had progressed from a modest contract p

item.title: Ebola Bundibugyo Outbreak in DRC and Uganda: Regional Risk High, Global Risk Low
item.description: A new Ebola outbreak involving the Bundibugyo strain has emerged in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and Uganda, presenting a high risk at the national and regional levels, but a low risk globally. The outbreak has resulted in at least 148 deaths and nearly 600 suspected cases, though exact numbers remain uncertain. The Bundibugyo strain has no specific treatment or vaccine, complicating containment efforts. The World Health Organization (WHO) declared the outbreak a public health emergency of international concern. Travel restrictions and monitoring measures are in place, and one American healthcare worker has tested positive and was transferred to Germany for treatment. Six high-risk contacts are being monitored in Europe. The outbreak likely began months ago, with a critical delay in detection, and the initial cases included healthcare workers who subsequently died. Ebola spreads through direct contact with infected bodily fluids and contaminated objects. While fatality rates

item.title: Walter Tyndale: Celebrated Watercolorist and Documenter of Architecture and Culture
item.description: Walter Tyndale (1855–1943) was a prominent British watercolorist known for his detailed and atmospheric depictions of architecture, landscapes, and street scenes. Born in Germany and later residing in England, he initially trained in classical painting techniques in Antwerp and Paris but eventually transitioned to watercolors, a medium that allowed him to capture light and shadow with great subtlety. Tyndale traveled extensively, visiting countries such as Egypt, Japan, Italy, and other parts of the Middle East, producing works that recorded both cultural vibrancy and architectural heritage. His paintings are characterized by precise topographical accuracy combined with a warm, artistic sensibility, which made him a sought-after illustrator for prestigious travel books, including 'Below the Cataracts' and 'An Artist in Egypt.' As a member of the Royal Institute of Painters in Water Colours (RI), Tyndale left behind a legacy of artworks that serve as both beautiful visual art and histor

item.title: The Life and Art of Georges Meunier, Celebrated Belle Époque Illustrator
item.description: Georges Meunier (1869–1942) was a renowned French poster artist and illustrator who vividly captured the lively spirit of Paris during the late 19th and early 20th centuries. A contemporary of Jules Chéret, Meunier became famous for transforming commercial art into an expression of joy and movement, departing from the rigid academic styles of his time. His illustrations often featured elegant Parisians enjoying leisure activities, rendered in soft pastel palettes with whimsical, fluid lines that reflected the optimism of the Belle Époque era. Meunier worked on a wide variety of projects, including advertisements for railway companies, liqueurs, perfumeries, and theatrical events. Notable works include 'Amara Blanqui, Le Meilleur Des Amers' (c. 1890), posters for Folies-Bergère and Nouveau Cirque, and promotional materials for American Crescent Cycles. Today, Meunier's lithographs are considered essential artifacts of the period, embodying a time of artistic innovation, prosperity, and

item.title: SMILE Satellite Launched to Observe Sun-Earth Interactions and Auroras
item.description: The SMILE (Solar wind Magnetosphere Ionosphere Link Explorer) satellite, a joint mission between the European Space Agency (ESA) and China's Academy of Sciences, was successfully launched on May 19, 2026, aboard the Vega C rocket from the Guiana Space Center. Originally planned for April, the launch was delayed due to technical issues. After reaching a preliminary circular orbit of 706 km, SMILE will use its propulsion system to move to its operational highly elliptical orbit of 5,000 x 121,000 km with a 51-hour period. The 2,250 kg satellite is designed to study the interaction between the solar wind and Earth's magnetosphere and ionosphere. Equipped with four scientific instruments, including a soft X-ray telescope, ultraviolet imager, ion detector, and magnetometer, SMILE will capture images of auroras and monitor space weather phenomena over extended periods, up to 45 hours per orbit. The mission aims to improve understanding of magnetic reconnection, aurora responses to solar wind

item.title: White House Prepares AI Executive Order as Google Unveils New AI Products at I/O Conference
item.description: The White House is set to release a highly anticipated executive order on artificial intelligence as soon as tomorrow, according to sources. The order is expected to outline key policy directions on AI development, safety, and governance. In parallel, Google made significant announcements at its annual I/O developer conference, showcasing a range of new AI-powered products. One notable feature is a universal shopping cart that lets users add items from various merchant websites into a single cart, aiming to streamline online shopping experiences. The company is also deepening its investment in multimedia AI capabilities. Nilesh Jasani, CEO of GenInnov Global Innovation Fund, discussed in an interview the strategic reasons behind Google's focus on multimedia integration within its AI ecosystem. Additionally, antitrust expert Joel Thayer provided insights on how these new offerings might be evaluated in the ongoing Department of Justice litigation against Google. The developments highlig

item.title: Trump's Efforts to Punish Republican Lawmakers May Backfire on the GOP
item.description: President Donald Trump's recent focus on targeting Republican lawmakers he perceives as disloyal, including Sen. Bill Cassidy and Rep. Thomas Massie, could create long-term challenges for his party, according to MSNBC's Nicolle Wallace. Political strategist Dan Pfeiffer noted that while Trump can influence primaries, spending large sums to defeat Republicans in districts he already won decisively may be a Pyrrhic victory. Wallace emphasized that Trump's aggressive strategy may force Republicans to distance themselves from him, as his unpopularity could drag the party down in the 2026 midterms. Moreover, Trump's actions are creating a group of 'lame-duck' Republicans who now oppose him openly, with Cassidy leading by joining Democrats on key votes and criticizing Trump's endorsed candidates. Other lawmakers, including Senate Majority Leader John Thune and Senator John Kennedy, have resisted Trump's directives, signaling growing internal resistance. Wallace suggests that this trend may e

item.title: Trump Endorses Ken Paxton, Leaving Senator John Cornyn Politically Vulnerable
item.description: Former GOP strategist Stuart Stevens expressed deep concern over U.S. Senator John Cornyn's political situation following a surprising endorsement of Cornyn's primary opponent, Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, by former President Donald Trump. Speaking on Harry Litman's podcast, Stevens said he could hardly recognize Cornyn, describing him as 'defeated' and 'a sad case.' Stevens suggested that Trump's endorsement was likely based on polling data showing Paxton ahead, emphasizing that Trump prefers to back perceived winners. Stevens contrasted Cornyn's situation with that of Rep. Thomas Massie, noting that Massie 'went down fighting' while Cornyn appeared demoralized. The article highlights the challenges faced by Cornyn, who previously attempted to appease Trump to secure his political standing, a strategy Stevens notes as ultimately unsuccessful. Cornyn's struggle reflects broader GOP dynamics, where Trump's influence continues to shape candidate viability, leaving some established

item.title: Jan. 6 Officers Sue to Block Trump Administration's $1.8 Billion Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description: Two police officers injured during the January 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol filed a lawsuit on May 20, 2026, against the Justice Department seeking to dissolve the newly created $1.8 billion Anti-Weaponization Fund. Former U.S. Capitol Police Officer Harry Dunn and Metropolitan Police Officer Daniel Hodges argue that the fund, established as part of a settlement in President Trump's $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS, violates the 14th Amendment by effectively aiding those involved in the insurrection. The officers claim it could provide financial payouts to far-right extremists, paramilitary groups like the Proud Boys and Oath Keepers, and other January 6 participants who received pardons from Trump. The fund is designed to compensate individuals claiming they were victims of 'lawfare and weaponization' by previous administrations, including those who spent time in custody related to such cases. Dunn and Hodges highlight ongoing threats and harassment they face from Trump supp

item.title: Trump Signals Potential Challenge to Rep. Lauren Boebert After Epstein Files Dispute
