item.description: President Donald Trump has indicated that he might support a primary challenger against Republican Representative Lauren Boebert of Colorado after she broke ranks with him and allied with Representative Thomas Massie of Kentucky. Boebert recently opposed Trump's demands regarding the release of government documents connected to Jeffrey Epstein, including refusing to remove her name from a discharge petition to make the files public. Despite Massie's recent primary loss in a high-profile race, Boebert reaffirmed her support for Trump publicly. Trump told reporters that although it is late in the primary cycle, he would consider backing someone willing to challenge Boebert in the upcoming June 30 primary. This development follows reports that Boebert had defied Trump's direct ultimatum, which included a high-level meeting at the Situation Room. The incident highlights ongoing tensions within the Republican Party and signals possible internal battles leading up to the 2026 midterm electio

item.title: Daniel Dae Kim Finds 'Lost' Series Finale Satisfying Due to Character Closure
item.description: Actor Daniel Dae Kim, best known for his role as Jin-Soo Kwon in the acclaimed ABC series 'Lost,' recently shared his positive perspective on the show's highly debated 2010 series finale. In an interview with People magazine at the Gold Gala, the 57-year-old actor expressed satisfaction with the ending, emphasizing that what mattered most to him was the reunion of the characters in the afterlife church scene. This moment allowed the cast to say goodbye to one another on screen, mirroring their real-life connections forged during the six-season run. Kim highlighted his deep affection for the show, noting it created lifelong friendships and launched his career in ways no other project has. 'Lost' followed the survivors of Oceanic Flight 815 after their plane crashed on a mysterious Pacific island, blending elements of drama, science fiction, and mystery. The finale, which showed Jack Shephard reuniting with his fellow survivors after death to move on together, drew significant criticism

item.title: Celebrating Irene Vanbrugh: Icon of British Theatre
item.description: Dame Irene Vanbrugh (1872–1949) was a legendary figure in British theatre, admired for her extraordinary talent and commanding presence on stage. Over a career lasting more than fifty years, she became a favorite of leading playwrights of her era. Vanbrugh is especially known for originating the role of Gwendolen Fairfax in Oscar Wilde's celebrated 1895 play, The Importance of Being Earnest, and for her remarkable performances in works by Arthur Wing Pinero and J.M. Barrie. Her acting was characterized by a unique combination of wit, elegance, and emotional depth, enabling her to excel in both sophisticated comedies and serious dramas. Beyond her theatrical achievements, Vanbrugh dedicated significant efforts to charitable work within the arts. In recognition of her lifelong contribution, she was honored as a Dame Commander of the Order of the British Empire (DBE) in 1941. Her enduring legacy is also commemorated through the Vanbrugh Theatre at the Royal Academy of Dramatic Art (RADA),

item.title: Nicolas Cage’s Costume Tests for Tim Burton’s Cancelled Superman Film
item.description: In the mid-1990s, Warner Bros. planned a bold reboot of the Superman franchise called 'Superman Lives,' directed by Tim Burton and starring Nicolas Cage as Clark Kent. The film, inspired by the 'Death of Superman' comic storyline, was set to feature iconic villains such as Lex Luthor, Brainiac, and Doomsday. The plot involved Superman’s death at Doomsday’s hands and his subsequent resurrection through a high-tech Kryptonian suit known as The Eradicator. The proposed cast also included Sandra Bullock as Lois Lane, Chris Rock as Jimmy Olsen, and Christopher Walken as Brainiac. The production was infamous for producer Jon Peters’ unusual creative demands, including prohibiting Superman from flying, changing the classic costume, and ending the film with a battle against a giant spider. Despite $30 million spent on pre-production, including elaborate glowing suits, the film was canceled in 1998 due to budget concerns and the disappointing performance of other superhero films of the era. Whi

item.title: Amazon Ends Support for Older Kindle Devices: Options for Users
item.description: Amazon has announced that, as of May 20, 2026, many older Kindle devices will no longer be able to purchase, borrow, or download new content from the Kindle Store. This includes almost all Kindle models released before 2013, such as the Kindle 1st and 2nd generation, Kindle DX, Kindle Keyboard, and early Kindle Fires. While these devices will still power on and allow access to previously downloaded books, new content will no longer be available directly through Amazon. Users can prepare by downloading as many books as possible before support ends. Some may consider sideloading or jailbreaking their devices to access new content, though these actions likely violate Amazon's terms of service. For those unwilling to take these risks, the best alternative is to upgrade to a newer e-reader. While losing direct access to the Kindle Store may be frustrating for longtime users, older Kindles can still serve as functional e-readers for preloaded content. The update underscores Amazon’s shift to

item.title: Advocacy Group Finds Tracked Starbucks Cold Cups Did Not Reach Recycling Facilities
item.description: Starbucks recently announced that its cold-beverage cups have earned a 'widely recyclable' designation, suggesting expanded access to curbside recycling nationwide. However, an investigation by the advocacy group Beyond Plastics challenges this claim. According to the group, none of the Starbucks cold cups they tracked using special devices actually made it to a recycling facility. The findings highlight ongoing issues with plastic recycling in the United States, particularly for items like polypropylene cups used by the coffee giant. Starbucks has pushed back against the report, criticizing the methodology of the probe. The company noted that the tracking devices attached to the cups are made of non-recyclable materials, which may have influenced how the items were sorted and processed at waste facilities. This discrepancy raises questions about corporate sustainability claims versus real-world recycling outcomes. Environmental advocates argue that many plastics labeled as recyclable

item.title: The History and Legacy of Manchester's Peveril of the Peak Pub
item.description: Peveril of the Peak, affectionately called 'The Pev' by locals, is one of Manchester's most iconic Victorian-era pubs. Established around 1820, it is located on a distinctive triangular plot on Great Bridgewater Street and is now a Grade II listed building. Its vibrant green and yellow ceramic tiles and stained-glass windows, added during a remodeling around 1900, make it a visually striking landmark. The origin of its name is debated, with some linking it to Sir Walter Scott's novel and others to a famous Manchester-to-London stagecoach. Despite urban development clearing the surrounding buildings, The Pev has remained standing, maintaining its historic charm. The pub has been managed by the Swanick family since 1971, with landlady Nancy Swanick being a legendary figure in Manchester's pub scene. Over the years, the pub has accumulated many colorful stories, including reports of paranormal activity and its use as a brothel by stationed G.I.s during World War II. It has also hosted cel

item.title: Chris Rabb Secures Democratic Nomination in Pennsylvania’s 3rd District
item.description: Chris Rabb, a progressive state representative known for his outspoken and activist style, won the Democratic primary for Pennsylvania’s 3rd Congressional District, a seat long held by Democrat Dwight Evans. Rabb’s victory marks a significant moment for the progressive wing of the party and represents a challenge to Philadelphia’s traditional political establishment. In a race with several contenders claiming progressive credentials, Rabb positioned himself furthest to the left, earning endorsements from high-profile progressive figures, including members of the Congressional 'Squad,' and support from organizations like the Working Families Party and the Democratic Socialists of America. Rabb’s campaign emphasized strong stances on issues such as Israel’s actions in Gaza, drawing sharp contrasts with his opponents and attempting to link them to influential lobbying groups, though the targeted organization denied involvement. His triumph reflects broader national trends in the Democrati

item.title: Climate Conditions and Rodent Populations Linked to Hantavirus Outbreak in Argentina
item.description: The recent hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship highlights the growing public health risks associated with climate-driven ecological changes. In Argentina and Chile, wetter-than-usual conditions, partly influenced by El Niño, have triggered explosive population booms of long-tailed pygmy rice rats, known locally as 'ratadas.' These rodents are carriers of hantavirus, including the Andes virus, which can spread from rodents to humans and, uniquely, between humans. Increased rainfall and vegetation growth have provided abundant food sources, allowing rodent populations to grow and reproduce rapidly. As rodents compete for territory and resources, aggressive behaviors facilitate viral transmission through bites and saliva, while infected rodents contaminate the environment with urine and feces. Changing land use and human encroachment into natural habitats are also increasing opportunities for human exposure. Cases have been rising, with over 100 confirmed infections in centr

item.title: Thomas Massie Loses Kentucky Primary but Builds Potential Anti-Trump Faction Within GOP
item.description: Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) lost his Republican primary election on May 19, 2026, to Trump-endorsed candidate Ed Gallrein, a former Navy SEAL. The defeat highlights President Donald Trump's strong influence over the Republican Party as he continues to target critics within the GOP. Despite the loss, a Slate analysis by Ben Jacobs suggests Massie may be forming the foundation of a new 'loud minority' coalition that could challenge the Trumpist establishment in the future. At Massie's election night watch party in Kentucky, attendees included a diverse group: local supporters, national influencers, libertarians heading to their party's convention, former Rep. Dana Rohrabacher, and even Proud Boys founder Gavin McInnes. The crowd chanted 'America First' and 'No War,' evoking comparisons to the Ron Paul and Tea Party movements of the past. Massie has been a vocal critic of certain Trump administration policies, notably pushing for the full release of Jeffrey Epstein files through the Epstein

item.title: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. comments on teenage mental health and psychiatric drugs
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. sparked concern during an episode of his government podcast, 'The Secretary Kennedy Podcast,' by making an unsolicited comment about being frequently around teenage girls and their mental health. During the discussion with Laura Delano, author and mental health advocate, Kennedy suggested that young girls often face emotional turbulence, and he expressed caution about prescribing psychiatric medications during this critical developmental period. Delano supported Kennedy's observations, noting that psychiatric diagnoses can sometimes become part of a young person's identity and may mask underlying needs for guidance and understanding. Kennedy launched the podcast in April, promoting it as a platform for radical transparency in government. Critics, however, have raised concerns that, because the show is produced under an HHS-branded studio, it could undermine public trust in federal health agencies. Kennedy oversees agencies such as the FDA and NIH,

item.title: Behind the Scenes of Heath Ledger’s Joker Performance in The Dark Knight
item.description: Heath Ledger’s portrayal of the Joker in the opening bank heist scene of The Dark Knight (2008) demonstrated meticulous preparation and set a new benchmark for action sequences. Director Christopher Nolan filmed this scene first so Ledger could gradually inhabit the character, since the Joker was masked for most of the sequence. This allowed Ledger to experiment with his physical presence before the unmasked reveal. The sequence was groundbreaking as it marked the first time IMAX 70mm cameras were used for a narrative dramatic scene, requiring precise pre-visualization due to the cameras’ size and noise. Iconic moments, such as the Joker removing his mask and delivering his line about resilience and madness, were slightly out of focus because of these technical challenges, yet Nolan retained the take due to Ledger’s compelling performance. The Gotham National Bank exterior was a constructed set in Chicago, while the interior was filmed at the Old Chicago Main Post Office. Stunts, inclu

item.title: Inside Lennixx: Creative Process, Music Style, and Plans for 2026
item.description: Lennixx, an emerging music duo, is gaining attention for their adventurous approach to creating and sharing music. Rejecting conventional methods, they embrace experimentation, curiosity, and transparency in the studio. Their TikTok challenges, once unfamiliar territory, have become a signature part of their creative process, allowing fans to engage with the behind-the-scenes development of their music. Known for blending alternative pop with energetic, cheeky vibes, Lennixx delivers music that feels both fresh and cinematic. 'Minute' is highlighted as a standout track, capturing the duo's mix of confidence and charm. Looking ahead, Lennixx plans to expand their reach with new collaborations, live performances, and innovative visuals, all while keeping the music at the forefront. Balancing friendship and professional collaboration is central to their dynamic, as they navigate the interplay between casual hangouts and focused studio sessions. The duo emphasizes honesty and fun in both w

item.title: Michael Sherard and His Impact on Post-War British Fashion
item.description: Michael Sherard (1910–1998) was a leading British fashion designer who played a pivotal role in reestablishing London as a global fashion capital after World War II. A member of the elite Incorporated Society of London Fashion Designers, Sherard was celebrated for his romantic-classical aesthetic and exceptional tailoring skills. He specialized in evening gowns and formal wear for the British aristocracy, using luxurious fabrics and intricate details such as his signature leafy-tiered skirts. Beyond fashion, he contributed to the performing arts, designing costumes for around 30 West End productions, including the original 1952 staging of Agatha Christie's The Mousetrap. Sherard’s work was widely photographed and featured in leading publications such as Harper’s Bazaar UK and Vogue, showcasing models like Barbara Goalen, Anne Gunning, and Jean Shrimpton in his iconic creations. After closing his fashion house in 1964, he transitioned to academia, mentoring the next generation of design

item.title: Meghan Markle Confirms She Will Not Return to Suits
item.description: Meghan Markle has officially addressed speculation about a possible return to the hit legal drama Suits, confirming that she will not reprise her role as Rachel Zane. Rumors had emerged following comments from series creator Aaron Korsh, who mentioned a potential Suits movie, leading fans to assume that original cast members, including Markle, might return. Her representatives quickly clarified that she has no plans to return to acting or revisit the Suits universe. Meghan left the series in 2017 after becoming engaged to Prince Harry, marking a major career and personal life shift. Since then, she has focused on her new responsibilities and priorities, emphasizing that her departure from acting was a permanent decision rather than a temporary break. While the Suits franchise continues to grow in popularity with spin-offs and streaming success, insiders agree that Meghan’s involvement is highly unlikely. The clarification has ended fan debates and speculation, confirming that any futur

item.title: Former Federal Prosecutor Indicted for Attempting to Steal Trump Investigation Report
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice has charged Carmen Lineberger, 62, a former managing assistant U.S. attorney in the Southern District of Florida, for allegedly attempting to steal a sealed report on President Donald Trump by disguising it as a cake recipe. The report was part of special counsel Jack Smith's investigation into classified documents found at Trump's Mar-a-Lago residence. According to the indictment, Lineberger received copies of the report in January 2025 and subsequently emailed them to her personal accounts using file names like 'Chocolate_Cake_Recipe.pdf' and 'Bundt_Cake_Recipe.pdf' to conceal their true content. Lineberger pleaded not guilty to charges including obstruction of justice and theft of government records. If convicted on all counts, she could face up to 25 years in prison, although federal sentencing guidelines often result in shorter terms. The case highlights concerns about the handling of sensitive government investigations and the security measures surr

item.title: HuffPost 2026 Shopping Award Winners Now on Memorial Day Sale
item.description: HuffPost has announced the winners of its 2026 Shopping Awards, highlighting 74 top-rated products across tech, home, beauty, pet care, and fashion. Many of these award-winning items are currently available at discounted prices for Memorial Day. Notable products include Danner Mountain 600 Leaf boots, praised for their durability, waterproofing, and stylish versatility, now up to 55% off; Active mold stain remover gel, a top-rated household cleaner at 20% off; and Shark's cordless Clean & Empty and Pet vacuums, which offer adaptability, long battery life, and strong performance, now up to 28% off. Other highlighted products include the Vornado Pedestal VFan, known for powerful air circulation; the iRestore red light therapy helmet for hair growth; Yuyqa ultrasonic dog bark deterrent device; Gap's oversized cotton sweater; Merrell Jungle Moc shoes; and the Oneisall dog water fountain. Each product was selected for its quality, customer reviews, and usability, offering practical solution

item.title: Desingerica criticizes Eurovision, calls competition unfair despite liking the winning song
item.description: Serbian rapper and artist Desingerica openly criticized this year's Eurovision Song Contest, calling it unfair and rigged. Speaking during the filming of the music competition show 'Pinkove zvezde,' Desingerica expressed that while he enjoyed the winning song and had predicted its victory even before the final, he has no interest in participating in Eurovision himself. He emphasized that the contest does not guarantee long-term success or popularity, arguing that winning a competition song does not necessarily translate into a sustainable music career or revenue. Desingerica also stated that although he has the talent to perform at Eurovision, the atmosphere and the perceived lack of fairness deter him from competing. He described the competition as a 'devilish job' and insisted that it is beneath his honor to participate. Despite his criticism, Desingerica acknowledged the quality of this year’s winning entry, contrasting it with other performances that, in his opinion, lacked distinc

item.title: Many GOP voters in Kentucky rely solely on Trump's endorsement, unaware of candidates’ records
item.description: In the recent election in Kentucky, a significant number of Republican voters who supported Ed Gallrein over incumbent Rep. Thomas Massie demonstrated limited knowledge about the candidates' political records. Reporting for MS NOW, Mychael Schnell spoke with voters at polling places and found that many were unable to cite specific votes or policy positions that influenced their choice. Some voters even believed falsely that Massie had voted to impeach former President Trump, a claim that Schnell clarified as incorrect. Despite attempts to probe further into policy disagreements, voters frequently admitted that their primary motivation was following Trump's endorsement. Several voters openly stated that they were voting for Gallrein because Trump recommended it, even if they did not personally like Gallrein or were unaware of his policy stances. This trend highlights the strong influence of presidential endorsements on voter behavior, as well as the potential spread of misinformation im

item.title: The Iconic Heart-and-Dagger Sweaters of 1940s Hollywood
item.description: In the 1940s, the 'heart-and-dagger' or 'bleeding heart' sweater became a notable trend in novelty knitwear, largely popularized by Los Angeles designer Suse (Susan) Dannenberg. Seeking to draw attention away from her figure, Dannenberg applied intricate felt appliqués, embroidery, and sometimes beads or sequins to hand-knit sweaters, creating playful and eye-catching designs. Hollywood starlets like Barbara Stanwyck and Esther Williams frequently wore these sweaters, and the style even gained coverage in LIFE magazine. The most famous version, known as the 'Jezebel' sweater, featured a bejeweled heart-and-dagger motif often shown as a bleeding or pierced heart. Other variations included hearts pierced by arrows, a design reminiscent of Ginger Rogers' attire in a 1938 film. Typically, these sweaters had high, rounded crew necks, wide shoulders, and slightly cropped, fitted waists. They exemplified the whimsical side of 1940s fashion, offering a contrast to the era's practical, military

item.title: DOJ Civil Rights Division sends election records request to wrong Michigan office in 2024 probe
item.description: The Department of Justice's Civil Rights Division, led by Assistant Attorney General Harmeet Dhillon, has faced criticism after sending a records demand related to Detroit's 2024 election results to the wrong local office. According to reports, the request was sent to Wayne County's clerk, even though elections in Michigan are administered by cities and townships rather than county clerks. Local officials expressed bemusement at the error, with one comparing it to ordering the wrong item at a fast-food restaurant, highlighting a perceived lack of basic understanding of Michigan's election structure. The investigation focuses on the 2024 election, which Donald Trump won in Michigan, raising questions about the probe's purpose among election veterans. Former Michigan elections director Chris Thomas noted significant improvements in operations since 2020 and welcomed scrutiny of 2024 processes. The incident is part of broader Trump administration efforts examining elections, including act

item.title: DNA Confirms 11,000-Year-Old Human Remains in Northern Britain Belonged to a Young Girl
item.description: Archaeologists have identified the oldest known human remains in Northern Britain as those of a young girl who lived approximately 11,000 years ago. The remains were discovered in Heaning Wood Bone Cave, near Great Urswick in Cumbria, during excavations led by self-taught local archaeologist Martin Stables. Known locally as the “Ossick Lass,” the child is estimated to have been between 2.5 and 3.5 years old at the time of her death. DNA analysis conducted by an international research team led by the University of Central Lancashire confirmed her sex and provided precise age details, marking a significant breakthrough in Mesolithic archaeology. The cave also contained other burials and artifacts, including a pierced deer tooth and beads, suggesting it held spiritual importance for early hunter-gatherer communities. These findings place the burial among the earliest known Mesolithic burials in northwestern Europe and offer rare insight into post-Ice Age human activity in northern Britain

item.title: Ann-Margret: Iconic Actress and Singer Who Defined 1960s Hollywood
item.description: Ann-Margret emerged as one of the most dynamic and talented performers of the 1960s, captivating audiences with her vibrant energy, powerful voice, and magnetic screen presence. Often dubbed the 'female Elvis Presley,' she rose to fame through memorable roles that blended singing, dancing, and acting. Her standout performance in the 1963 musical Bye Bye Birdie highlighted her ability to embody the youthful spirit of the era, while her chemistry with Elvis Presley in the 1964 film Viva Las Vegas cemented her status as a major star. Known for her striking red hair and charismatic style, Ann-Margret brought both glamour and depth to her work. Beyond the high-energy musicals that defined much of her early career, she demonstrated remarkable dramatic range in later decades. In the 1970s, she earned critical acclaim and two Academy Award nominations for her roles in Carnal Knowledge and the rock opera Tommy. Her career has spanned over six decades, showcasing her versatility as she transitio

item.title: House Democrats Move to Subpoena Trump Administration Officials Over $1.776 Billion Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description: House Democrats, led by Rep. Jamie Raskin of Maryland, have moved quickly to increase oversight and pressure on the Trump administration regarding the newly established $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund.' The fund aims to compensate individuals who claim they were politically targeted or unfairly prosecuted by previous administrations. Raskin, the top Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee, proposed subpoenas for acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, IRS CEO Frank Bisignano, and Treasury General Counsel Brian Morrissey during a committee hearing. Although Republicans hold enough votes to block the subpoenas, the move is seen as politically damaging for the GOP. Committee Chairman Jim Jordan agreed to hold a vote on the motion at the end of the hearing. In addition, Raskin and Ways and Means Committee ranking member Richard Neal sent a letter demanding documents related to the fund, describing it as 'one of the most brazen acts of public corrup

item.title: Ongoing Tourist Damage to Europe's Historic Landmarks Raises Local Concerns
item.description: Across Europe, a growing number of incidents involving tourists damaging historic landmarks is alarming both residents and heritage experts. Visitors have been observed carving initials into iconic structures like the Colosseum in Rome, breaking historic fountains, and even harming timeless works of art. This behavior not only threatens the preservation of cultural heritage but also sparks frustration among locals who rely on tourism for economic support yet wish to see their heritage respected. Authorities in several European countries are calling for stricter enforcement of preservation laws and better awareness campaigns aimed at educating visitors about the consequences of such actions. Experts warn that without immediate intervention, these damages could lead to irreversible loss of cultural treasures that have stood for centuries. The trend highlights a conflict between mass tourism and heritage conservation, emphasizing the need for responsible travel practices. European museums

item.title: Madison Square Garden Bans Lawyer Representing Injured NYPD Officer Amid Legal Dispute
item.description: John Scola, an attorney known for representing NYPD officers, has been banned from Madison Square Garden (MSG) and other James Dolan-owned venues after filing a lawsuit on behalf of Officer John Przybyszewski. Przybyszewski alleges he suffered significant spinal injuries while working security at a boxing match in MSG's Hulu Theater in February 2025, due to insufficient staffing and chaotic crowd control during an incident involving rapper Lil Tjay. MSG’s facial recognition system, intended to prevent dangerous actors from entering, has reportedly been used to enforce bans on lawyers with ongoing disputes against the venue. This practice has drawn criticism from city officials, including New York Attorney General Letitia James and Mayor Zohran Mamdani, amid concerns about privacy violations and MSG security acting beyond its formal authority. While Przybyszewski hopes to return to paid detail work, including possibly at MSG, Scola has stated he remains committed to representing his cli

item.title: Severe thunderstorms expected to end record heat wave in Washington DC area
item.description: A severe thunderstorm watch has been issued for the Washington DC area as an intense cold front approaches, promising to bring an end to the current record-setting heat wave. Meteorologists from the Capital Weather Gang report that storms capable of producing damaging winds are likely on May 20, 2026. Following the passage of the cold front, temperatures are expected to drop significantly, bringing much cooler and damper conditions to the region. The scorching heat experienced today will give way to more comfortable temperatures in the coming days. This weather shift marks a dramatic change after several days of unusually high temperatures that have set records. Residents are advised to stay informed about potential severe weather, including strong winds and heavy rain that could accompany the thunderstorms. The cold front will also bring increased humidity and overcast skies typical after such frontal passages. Capital Weather Gang will continue providing live updates throughout the e

item.title: Eva Gabor's Hollywood Career and Style in the 1950s
item.description: Eva Gabor, born on February 11, 1919, in Hungary, became a celebrated Hungarian-American actress and socialite. During the 1950s, she established herself as a versatile performer in Hollywood and on Broadway. Unlike her sisters Zsa Zsa and Magda, who frequently attracted attention for their social lives, Eva focused on developing a solid acting career. Her breakthrough came with the critically acclaimed performance in 'The Happy Time' in 1950, which led to a Life Magazine cover. Eva also became one of the first women to host her own talk show, 'The Eva Gabor Show,' airing from 1953 to 1954. Throughout the decade, she appeared in several notable films, often in glamorous supporting roles, including 'The Last Time I Saw Paris,' 'Artists and Models,' 'My Man Godfrey,' 'Gigi,' and 'It Started with a Kiss.' Known for her warm personality and maintained Hungarian accent, Eva projected a high-fashion and sophisticated image. She would later parody this persona in her iconic role as Lisa Dougl

item.title: Filipino-American Bowler Family Portrait from the 1920s During U.S. Occupation of the Philippines
item.description: This historical family portrait from around 1920 captures William Leslie Bowler, an American, alongside his Filipina wife, Dolores Alcantara, and several of their children during the period of U.S. colonial administration in the Philippines. The image provides a rare glimpse into interracial marriages and multicultural family life at a time when such unions were relatively uncommon amid the geopolitical context of American influence in the archipelago following the Spanish-American War. The couple had nine children in total, blending American and Filipino heritage. Their daughter Josephine “Jo” Bowler, born in 1917 in Legaspi, is featured in the photo as an infant held by her older sister. Jo later became a teacher and mother, eventually moving to Montebello, California, where she passed away in 1988 at age 70. Other siblings included Joseph A., Mary Lourdes, William “Bill,” Michael “Mickey,” James “Jimmy,” Frank, John Edward, and Dolores “Dolly.” William Leslie Bowler died in 1930 at

item.title: Cher in the 1960s: Early Career, Style, and Rise to Fame
item.description: Before becoming a global pop icon and Academy Award winner, Cher, born Cheryl Sarkisian on May 20, 1946, emerged as a defining figure of 1960s counterculture. At just 16, she met Sonny Bono in Los Angeles and started as a backup singer for legendary producer Phil Spector. By 1965, Cher and Bono formed the duo Sonny & Cher, achieving instant success with their chart-topping hit 'I Got You Babe.' Their music resonated with the youth of the decade, blending folk-rock harmonies with socially progressive themes. Alongside her duo work, Cher launched a solo career, releasing the dramatic pop-folk hit 'Bang Bang (My Baby Shot Me Down)' in 1966, showcasing her individual star power. Fashion-wise, Cher became a trendsetter, popularizing bell-bottom pants, crop tops, and fur vests, while her signature makeup and straight black hair influenced a generation of young women. As the 1960s ended, shifts in musical trends temporarily affected the duo's popularity, leading them to focus on live comedy a

item.title: Georgia Supreme Court Maintains Republican Majority After Judicial Elections
item.description: In Georgia's recent judicial elections, two Democratic candidates, Miracle Rankin and former state Senator Jen Jordan, were unsuccessful in their attempts to unseat incumbent Republican Supreme Court Justices Charles Bethel and Sarah Warren. Both Republican justices had been appointed by GOP governors. While Georgia's judicial elections are officially nonpartisan, party affiliations still influence support and campaign strategies. Despite recent Democratic successes in other state elections and a higher voter turnout for their candidates in this race, Rankin and Jordan were unable to break the historical trend: no incumbent justice has lost reelection in Georgia in the past 100 years. The Democrats campaigned on progressive platforms supporting voting rights, civil rights, and abortion rights, but faced criticism from Republicans, including allegations of violating judicial conduct rules. A district court recently blocked the release of these findings, citing violations of free speech

item.title: EPIC Report Highlights Failures in Data Brokers and AI Firms’ Opt-Out Processes
item.description: A recent report by the Electronic Privacy Information Center (EPIC) reveals that 38 major data-collecting companies in the U.S.—including AI vendors, data brokers, defense contractors, and dating apps—employ manipulative practices that hinder consumers from opting out of data collection and sharing. The study identifies multiple tactics such as buried links, multiple forms, account or subscription requirements, and misleading toggles. High-profile companies like Google, Meta, OpenAI, Amazon, and Palantir were scrutinized for failing to provide clear, accessible opt-out options. People-search sites like Spokeo, Whitepages, and National Public Data offer limited removal options but continue selling personal data, raising safety concerns highlighted by past criminal cases. EPIC argues that opt-out mechanisms are often insufficient for protecting vulnerable populations, including domestic violence survivors, women of color, LGBTQ+ individuals, and public officials. The report concludes tha

item.title: Jennifer Aniston’s 1997 Tatler Magazine Photoshoot by Matthew Rolston
item.description: In August 1997, Jennifer Aniston appeared in the British edition of Tatler Magazine during a pivotal time in her career. At the height of her fame from the hit TV show Friends, where she portrayed the beloved character Rachel Green, Aniston was transitioning toward a film career. That year, she was promoting her romantic comedy Picture Perfect and featured on other major magazine covers. The Tatler photoshoot, captured by renowned celebrity photographer Matthew Rolston in Los Angeles, showcased a more sophisticated and glamorous 'Old Hollywood' aesthetic, diverging from her approachable, girl-next-door image on Friends. Rolston's signature polished lighting style highlighted Aniston's elegance and star power. The feature emphasized her growth from television star to potential leading lady in Hollywood. By this point, Aniston was also dealing with intense media scrutiny, especially as her personal life, including her budding high-profile relationship with Brad Pitt, began attracting tab

item.title: Researchers Identify Enzyme IDOL as a Potential Target for Alzheimer’s Treatment
item.description: Researchers at Indiana University School of Medicine have identified an enzyme called IDOL as a potential new target for treating Alzheimer's disease. Laboratory experiments demonstrated that removing IDOL from neurons significantly reduced amyloid plaques, a key hallmark of the disease, and improved brain processes associated with neuron communication and resilience. Surprisingly, while microglia—brain immune cells—produce IDOL, removing it from neurons had the most pronounced effect. The intervention also reduced levels of apolipoprotein E (APOE), particularly APOE4, a major genetic risk factor for late-onset Alzheimer's, and increased receptors involved in regulating amyloid plaques and lipid metabolism. These findings suggest that targeting neuronal IDOL could simultaneously decrease plaque burden and enhance neuroprotective mechanisms, offering a therapeutic strategy that goes beyond current treatments like lecanemab and donanemab, which primarily slow disease progression. Future

item.title: Trump Supports Flavored Vapes Amid Political and Industry Interests
item.description: Former President Donald Trump has taken steps to expand access to flavored e-cigarettes, signaling strong support for the vaping community. Earlier in his administration, Trump initially planned to ban most flavored e-cigarettes to curb youth vaping but retreated after pushback from adult vapers and industry stakeholders. Recently, under apparent pressure from Trump, the FDA authorized the sale of blueberry- and mango-flavored vape juices from the company Glas, marking the first approval of non-tobacco, non-menthol flavors. Critics, including former FDA officials, argue this decision primarily benefits the vaping industry and adult users rather than addressing youth vaping. Trump’s approach appears consistent with his political strategy, aiming to appeal to niche voter groups and maintain favorable relationships with tobacco companies, which financially supported his campaigns. While studies suggest flavored vapes may help adult smokers transition from combustible cigarettes, the major

item.title: Behind the Scenes of the 1975 Epic The Man Who Would Be King Starring Sean Connery and Michael Caine
item.description: The Man Who Would Be King is a classic 1975 epic adventure film directed by John Huston, adapted from Rudyard Kipling’s 1888 novella. The movie stars Sean Connery as Daniel Dravot and Michael Caine as Peachy Carnehan, two ambitious former British Army sergeants who leave India to seek fortune in the remote region of Kafiristan (in modern-day Afghanistan). Believing the land untouched by white men since Alexander the Great, they use their military skills, rifles, and cunning to ally with local tribes, train an army, and eventually seize control. When Dravot survives an arrow wound unharmed, the locals proclaim him a god and the son of Alexander, granting him kingship and access to ancient treasures. The story explores themes of imperialism, hubris, and the corrupting influence of power as Dravot begins to believe his own legend, breaking a pact with Carnehan by deciding to marry a local woman named Roxanne, played by Shakira Caine. The revelation of his mortality leads to a tragic clima

item.title: Anonymous Donation Brings Significant Support to Family of Rottnest Shark Attack Victim
item.description: An anonymous donor contributed $100,000 to a GoFundMe campaign supporting the family of Steven Mattaboni, who tragically died in a shark attack off Rottnest Island. The generous donation brought the total raised to over $317,660 of the $500,000 target. Friends and community members have also donated, with contributions ranging from $1,000 to $10,000, to help Mattaboni’s wife, Shirene, and their two young daughters. The 38-year-old father was spearfishing near Horseshoe Reef when the attack occurred. Despite immediate CPR from friends and paramedics’ intervention, he could not be saved. Mattaboni was remembered as a devoted father, loving husband, experienced fisherman, and passionate spearfisherman. He also co-founded Casana Studio with his wife and worked as a surveyor. The local community, including the Kingsley Football Club where he played, has rallied around the family. The fundraiser aims to provide practical and emotional support during this incredibly difficult time. Friends an

item.title: CNN Fact-Checker Refutes Trump's False Claims About California Election Results
item.description: During a press appearance at Joint Base Andrews on May 20, 2026, President Donald Trump repeated false claims that the U.S. presidential elections in 2016, 2020, and 2024 were 'rigged' against him. When asked about a MAGA-backed Los Angeles mayoral candidate, Trump labeled California elections as 'dishonest' and exaggerated his support among Hispanic voters, claiming that even Jesus Christ counting votes would not have changed the outcome in his favor. CNN Senior Reporter Daniel Dale fact-checked these statements, noting that Trump lost California by wide margins in all three elections—30 points in 2016, 29 points in 2020, and 20 points in 2024—and emphasized that votes are counted accurately in every state. These claims are consistent with Trump's long-standing narrative that elections are manipulated against him, a viewpoint repeatedly rejected by courts and election officials. The incident highlights the ongoing tension between Trump and fact-checkers as he continues to make unverif

item.title: Light-Driven Method Produces Strained Housane Molecules for Drug Development
item.description: Researchers at the University of Münster, led by Prof. Frank Glorius, have developed a novel light-driven technique to create highly strained ring-shaped molecules called housanes. These tiny molecules, named for their resemblance to simple house drawings, are valuable for drug development and materials science because their internal tension can drive chemical reactions efficiently. Traditional methods of producing housanes often require harsh conditions like high temperatures and are limited in tolerating functional groups, which are critical for molecular behavior and properties. By using photocatalysis and carefully adjusting the molecular side chains of 1,4-diene starting materials, the team was able to suppress unwanted side reactions and guide the molecules into the desired compact, high-energy structures. Computer simulations helped the researchers understand the reaction mechanism and optimize the process. This new method makes the production of these challenging molecules more

item.title: Analyst John Heilemann sees potential Democratic midterm wave based on NYT/Siena poll showing Trump underwater with key voter groups
item.description: On Wednesday, political analyst John Heilemann appeared on MSNBC's 'Morning Joe' to discuss the latest New York Times/Siena College poll, which shows President Donald Trump's approval rating at a record low of 37%. Heilemann highlighted particularly troubling numbers for Republicans ahead of the 2026 midterms, noting that Trump is significantly underwater with several key demographics. Among young voters, Trump is down by 59 points, and with independent voters, he trails by 43 points. The poll also shows a 14-point lead for Democrats on the generic congressional ballot. Heilemann pointed out that these figures, especially Trump's poor performance with Hispanics and independents, suggest a potential wave election building for Democrats. While he stopped short of firm predictions, Heilemann indicated that the data points to Democrats having a strong chance of a significant victory in November, potentially a 'tsunami' if the trends hold. The analysis comes amid ongoing discussions about R

item.title: Jim Jordan questions SPLC funding practices; civil rights attorney defends donor support
item.description: During a House Judiciary Committee hearing, Representative Jim Jordan (R-OH) challenged the Southern Poverty Law Center (SPLC) over its use of donor funds, focusing on allegations that paid informants featured on the SPLC's 'extremist of the month' page received $140,000 while promoting extremist groups. Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche framed the hearing as 'Manufacturing Hate,' criticizing the SPLC for allegedly fostering extremism. SPLC supporters, however, contend the charges are politically motivated, pointing out that their informants assist law enforcement in curbing violent extremist activity. Civil rights attorney Maya Wiley, representing the Leadership Conference on Civil and Human Rights, repeatedly deflected Jordan's line of questioning, emphasizing that donors continue to support the SPLC despite the controversy. When Jordan pressed Wiley on whether the funding strategy was appropriate, she responded that the donors have spoken through their continued contributions. Ob

item.title: Jan. 6 Officers File Lawsuit Against Trump-Linked Fund for Capitol Rioters
item.description: Two prominent police officers from the January 6, 2021, Capitol attack have filed a federal lawsuit challenging the Trump administration's $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund.' Former Capitol Police Officer Harry Dunn and Metropolitan Police Officer Daniel Hodges are seeking to prevent the fund from disbursing any money to rioters who assaulted them during the Capitol attack. The officers’ complaint describes the fund as illegal, claiming it violates the Constitution and federal law, particularly the 14th Amendment, which prohibits using federal funds to support insurrection. The fund was created as part of a settlement of Trump's $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS regarding leaked tax returns. The officers argue that the lawsuit itself was a strategic move with no legal merit and that the fund could put them in further danger by providing financial support to individuals who have threatened them. Both Dunn and Hodges continue to receive death threats linked to the events of Janua

item.title: How 1920s Women Embraced Cars as Symbols of Independence
item.description: In the 1920s, societal norms underwent dramatic changes, and the automobile emerged as a powerful symbol of freedom for women. No longer restricted to domestic roles or dependent on male drivers, women of this era confidently took the wheel, often photographed alongside sleek Model Ts or stylish roadsters. The decade saw the rise of the “New Woman,” who challenged traditional expectations through fashion, behavior, and mobility. Sporting bobbed hair, flapper dresses, and practical driving attire, women demonstrated autonomy both on the road and in society. These images captured the spirit of adventure, defiance, and modernity that defined the Jazz Age. Posing with cars became a visual statement of empowerment, signaling women’s entry into the broader public sphere. The act of driving was not merely practical but emblematic of social progress, personal freedom, and evolving gender roles. Through these photographs and cultural shifts, the 1920s showcased women actively shaping their dest

item.title: 1982 Renault 5 TL Found Unused in a French Barn After 43 Years
item.description: In an extraordinary automotive discovery, a 1982 Renault 5 TL has been found completely unused in a barn in Chalon-sur-Saône, France. The car, purchased new by Mrs. M in October 1982, was stored immediately upon delivery and never driven due to her hesitation to get behind the wheel, despite later obtaining her driver's license. Painted in the rare 'Shale Blue Metallic,' this five-door model features an 1108cc engine and a 4-speed gearbox. Remarkably, the car's odometer has remained frozen at just 12 km (7.45 miles) over the past 43 years. During probate proceedings, the vehicle was nearly lost, but fortunately it was preserved and is now set to be auctioned by the French house Aguttes on March 15, 2026, in Paris. Experts estimate an initial auction value between 5,000 and 10,000 euros, though the pristine condition of this 'barn find' could attract much higher bids. The discovery has sparked excitement among vintage car enthusiasts and collectors, highlighting the unique appeal of a v

item.title: Senator Chris Van Hollen Challenges Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche During Senate Hearing
item.description: During a recent episode of 'Jimmy Kimmel Live!' ABC host Jimmy Kimmel shared a notable exchange from the Senate Appropriations Committee, eliciting cheers from the live audience. The clip featured Senator Chris Van Hollen (D-MD) questioning acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, who previously served as Donald Trump’s personal lawyer. Van Hollen pressed Blanche on the oversight of a $1.776 billion fund for January 6 supporters, highlighting potential conflicts of interest given Blanche's past connections to Trump. Blanche insisted that his prior role as Trump’s lawyer was irrelevant to his current position, while Van Hollen countered that this relationship could compromise the independence of the fund's administration. Kimmel described Van Hollen’s pointed remarks as a “perfect comeback,” likening it to an ideal retort one imagines in private. The segment drew laughter and applause from the audience, emphasizing public interest in political accountability and transparency. The interacti

item.title: Raja’s Appam: A Small Taman Melawati Eatery Serving Fluffy, Flavorful Appams
item.description: Located in Taman Melawati, Raja’s Appam has become a beloved local spot for traditional South Indian-style pancakes called appam. Originally operating as a catering service from 2017 to 2020, the business transitioned into a food truck in 2021 before settling into its current small eatery in March 2025. The restaurant has gained a reputation for its freshly made, crispy-edged, and spongy-centered appams, served with toppings such as banana, corn, egg, or brown sugar. The Banana Appam offers ripe bananas mixed with coconut milk and a sprinkle of brown sugar, while the Special Appam combines a soft-cooked egg with caramelized brown sugar. Other menu items include thosai, chapati, poori, idli, vadai, and curry puffs, all prepared fresh from scratch. Drinks like Teh Tarik complement the breakfast offerings perfectly. During weekends, customers should expect long waits due to the eatery’s popularity, but the quality and freshness of the dishes make the wait worthwhile. Raja’s Appam has succ

item.title: Sen. John Cornyn briefly shares and deletes post criticizing Trump ahead of Texas Senate runoff
item.description: Senator John Cornyn (R-TX) briefly shared a social media post labeling former President Donald Trump the 'Commander-in-Cheat' before quickly deleting it. The post, originating from the advocacy group Pastors for Children, criticized Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton for various scandals, including his impeachment, felony indictment, and divorce, and ended with a jab referencing Trump's endorsement. Cornyn added his own comment highlighting upcoming voter participation in the May 26 Republican Senate runoff. This incident occurred shortly after Trump announced his support for Paxton over Cornyn, marking a significant political challenge for the three-term senator. Earlier, Cornyn had acknowledged his long-standing cooperation with Trump, noting he voted in alignment with the former president more than 99% of the time. The runoff election is expected to be closely watched as early voting is already underway, with Cornyn trying to maintain support among Texas Republicans amid the high-pro

item.title: Daily Life and Culture in Japan During the 1920s
item.description: The 1920s in Japan, often referred to as the era of Taisho Democracy, was a period of significant cultural and social transformation. Cities like Tokyo and Osaka experienced rapid Westernization, reflected in the rise of the 'Salaryman' workforce and the fashionable Modern Girls, who embraced bobbed hairstyles and flapper dresses. Jazz, cinema, and a consumer-driven urban lifestyle became symbols of this changing society. At the same time, Japan was recovering from the 1923 Great Kanto Earthquake, which triggered extensive industrial, architectural, and social reconstruction. Cafes in districts like Ginza became hubs for liberal ideas and labor movements, signaling a brief period of artistic and intellectual freedom. Despite these modernizing influences, traditional practices persisted, as seen in the wandering Komuso monks, religious sites like Kasuga-taisha Shrine, and everyday rural life. Vintage photos from this decade capture a range of activities, from children playing and famili

item.title: Fashionable Gentlemen and Their Groomed Dog in 1880s Stockholm
item.description: In 1880s Stockholm, two gentlemen walked the streets dressed in the era's finest fashion. Their top hats, tailored coats, and polished boots reflected both their social standing and the importance of personal appearance in urban life during late 19th-century Sweden. Accompanying them was a meticulously groomed dog, highlighting how pets served as both companions and symbols of status. The photograph captures more than just style; it offers a window into everyday life where civility, leisure, and social pride were expressed openly. Public appearances were carefully orchestrated, and the bond between humans and animals played a visible role in reinforcing social respectability. Observing such a scene today provides insight into the cultural and societal norms of the period, where refinement and etiquette were central to urban identity, and even simple walks became opportunities to demonstrate taste, attention to detail, and companionship.

item.title: Google Introduces AI Agents to Provide Continuous, Personalized Search Updates
item.description: At Google I/O 2026, Google unveiled a major evolution in Search with the introduction of agentic AI capabilities. These new AI agents allow users to create, customize, and manage multiple assistants that continuously monitor topics of interest. Unlike traditional search engines that respond only to queries, these agents operate 24/7, gathering information, analyzing perspectives, and providing actionable insights. Users can set up agents for a variety of tasks, including tracking stock market updates, monitoring flight prices, following sports events, or staying informed about news and trends. The agents go beyond notifications, synthesizing data from multiple sources and offering context on why information matters. To activate an agent, users enter a prompt in AI Mode, and the Google app sends push notifications when relevant updates occur. The new agents will first be available to Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers in the U.S., with a broader rollout planned later. Additionally, Goo

item.title: Polls Suggest Texas Senate Race Could Be Competitive for Democrats
item.description: Recent analysis by CNN analyst Harry Enten indicates that Democrats may have a genuine opportunity to win a Senate seat in Texas, a state long considered solidly Republican. This insight comes ahead of the May 26 runoff election, where President Donald Trump endorsed state attorney general Ken Paxton over incumbent Senator John Cornyn. Enten's review of polling data shows James Talarico, the Democratic candidate, leading Paxton in head-to-head matchups by an average of four points. This marks the highest polling for a Democrat in Texas in over two decades. Enten highlighted several factors contributing to this shift: Paxton's legal and ethical controversies, including past impeachment and felony charges, and his low favorability ratings, which contrast sharply with the relatively positive ratings of previous Republican candidates like Ted Cruz. Additionally, Trump's declining popularity in Texas further complicates Republican prospects. While this does not guarantee a Democratic victor

item.title: ‘Elephants in the Fog’ Explores the Lives and Challenges of Nepal’s Kinnar Community
item.description: Abinash Bikram Shah’s feature debut, 'Elephants in the Fog,' offers an intimate and compelling portrayal of Nepal’s Kinnar community, legally recognized as part of the country's third gender. Set in a remote village near a forest, the film follows Pirati, a middle-aged trans woman who serves as the matriarch of a house for transgender refugees. Through her interactions with her adopted daughters, including former sex worker Apsara, Shah explores themes of acceptance, love, and societal roles within the community. The film interweaves the everyday rituals of the Kinnar with broader social commentary on the conditional acceptance trans people experience in South Asia. When Apsara disappears, Pirati confronts both the indifference of law enforcement and the strict rules of her matriarchy, navigating a story of loss, desire, and survival. The cinematography captures the rural environment’s mystique, and performances, particularly from Pushpa Thing Lama as Pirati, bring authenticity and emo

item.title: Sen. Ted Cruz avoids answering questions on January 6 rioters during TMZ interview
item.description: During an interview with TMZ reporter Charlie Cotton, U.S. Senator Ted Cruz (R-TX) faced repeated questions about whether January 6 rioters, some of whom were convicted and later pardoned, should be eligible for funds from former President Trump's $1.776 billion 'anti-weaponization fund.' Cruz repeatedly deflected the questions, focusing instead on criticizing President Joe Biden's alleged use of the Department of Justice, which frustrated the reporter. Cotton attempted several times to get a direct response regarding the eligibility of January 6 rioters for the funds. In the end, Cruz avoided providing a clear answer, defending the rioters as 'peaceful protesters' and suggesting they were targeted by the Department of Justice. The encounter concluded with Cruz quietly leaving via an elevator, leaving the reporter without a definitive response. The interview highlights the ongoing partisan tensions surrounding the aftermath of January 6 and questions about government spending on politi

item.title: Kehillat Israel synagogue reopens, offering hope to fire-affected Palisades community
item.description: Kehillat Israel, a synagogue in the Pacific Palisades, reopened last week for Shabbat services, becoming one of the first religious institutions to resume operations after the devastating fire that hit the area in January 2025. While the building itself did not burn, it suffered extensive smoke damage, and the reopening required both prior renovation efforts and post-fire remediation. Approximately 230 synagogue families lost their homes, with another 250 displaced, including the congregation’s rabbis. For many, the reopening provided an emotional anchor and a sense of normalcy amid ongoing challenges with rebuilding, insurance, and recovery. Congregants celebrated in a packed 400-person sanctuary, enjoying music, speeches, and a newly dedicated Torah ark honoring Cantor Chayim and Marcy Frenkel. The ark was designed to allow even young children to participate, reflecting the synagogue’s emphasis on community and accessibility. Local faith leaders and city officials attended the ceremo

item.title: A Historical Look at Anna Pavlova Through Vintage Postcards
item.description: Anna Pavlova, born Anna Matveyevna Pavlova on February 12, 1881, was a legendary Russian prima ballerina known for her poetic expression and delicate frame. Rising to the rank of Prima Ballerina in 1906 after her acclaimed performance in Giselle, she became internationally famous for her signature role in 'The Dying Swan,' choreographed by Michel Fokine in 1905. Pavlova briefly joined Sergei Diaghilev’s Ballets Russes in 1909 but soon formed her own company in 1911, becoming the first ballerina to tour the world extensively, performing across North and South America, Asia, and Australia. In 1912, she established her base in London at Ivy House, where she rehearsed and lived. Pavlova was known for her unconventional feet, which she adapted with innovative pointe shoes featuring hard leather soles and flattened boxes, a precursor to modern pointe shoes. Her 1926 tour of Australia and New Zealand left such an impact that the Pavlova dessert was named in her honor. Pavlova passed away from

item.title: Burt Reynolds: A Look at His Iconic Career and Style in the 1970s
item.description: Burt Reynolds, born Burton Leon Reynolds Jr. (1936–2018), was a defining figure in 1970s Hollywood, celebrated for his rugged charm, comedic timing, and physical performances. He rose to prominence through television roles in series like Gunsmoke, Hawk, and Dan August, before making a major impact in films such as Navajo Joe, 100 Rifles, and his breakout role as Lewis Medlock in the survival thriller Deliverance (1972). Reynolds became a cultural icon with his 1972 Cosmopolitan magazine cover, showcasing his charisma and solidifying his status as a heartthrob. In the mid to late 1970s, he dominated the box office with hits like Smokey and the Bandit, where he portrayed Bo 'Bandit' Darville, making both the character and the Pontiac Trans Am instantly legendary. He also drew on his sports background in films like The Longest Yard and Semi-Tough, combining athleticism with humor. Reynolds was known for his approachable personality and frequent appearances on The Tonight Show Starring Joh

item.title: Former Leader of Ex-Gay Ministry Arrested for Attempted Solicitation of Minor
item.description: Alan Chambers, 54, a former leader of the now-defunct 'ex-gay' ministry Exodus International, has been arrested in Florida for attempting to solicit sexual activity from someone he believed to be a 14-year-old boy. Chambers allegedly communicated with an undercover detective via Snapchat and Telegram from February through May 2026, sending sexually explicit messages and images while attempting to arrange a meeting. The Orange County Sheriff's Office charged him with solicitation of a minor via computer, transmission of material harmful to minors, and unlawful use of a two-way communication device. Chambers, who previously sparked controversy in the ex-gay movement by admitting that homosexuality could not be 'cured,' faces significant legal consequences. The Park Avenue District, where Chambers volunteered, announced his suspension from the board pending further review, emphasizing support for the community while respecting the legal process. This arrest follows a sting operation that

item.title: Elon Musk Criticizes Casting of Lupita Nyong’o in ‘The Odyssey,’ Citing Personal Beliefs
item.description: Elon Musk has drawn attention for his prolonged criticism of Lupita Nyong’o being cast as Helen of Troy in Christopher Nolan’s upcoming adaptation of Homer’s 'The Odyssey.' Musk, along with conservative commentators, expressed disapproval of casting a Black actress in a traditionally white role, framing it as a broader issue related to Western cultural heritage. Experts suggest that Musk’s reaction aligns with his known views on race and the so-called 'great replacement' theory, which posits that white populations are being systematically replaced by nonwhite populations. Political science professor Alvin B. Tillery explains that Musk’s criticism is less about film accuracy and more about his racial worldview, shaped by his upbringing in apartheid-era South Africa. Film scholar Kirsten Moana Thompson notes that Musk’s objections might also reflect traditionalist ideas about casting authenticity, though media representation continues to slowly diversify. The debate highlights tensions i

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Moments of James Dean and Elizabeth Taylor’s Double on the Set of 'Giant'
item.description: During the production of the 1956 epic film 'Giant' in Marfa, Texas, photographer Frank Worth captured a series of candid, behind-the-scenes images featuring James Dean and Valley Keene, Elizabeth Taylor’s stand-in. These photographs reveal a playful side of Dean, often remembered as moody and intense. One of the most iconic images shows Dean lifting Keene in an open field, highlighting the lighthearted moments that occurred between takes. The film’s primary outdoor scenes were shot at the Ryan Ranch in Marfa, while the Reata mansion was constructed as a three-sided façade specifically for filming. 'Giant' marked James Dean’s third and final leading role; tragically, he died in a car accident on September 30, 1955, shortly after finishing his scenes. The images of Dean with Keene offer a rare glimpse into the actor’s off-screen personality and the lighter moments behind a major Hollywood production.

item.title: Harry Styles Tour Team Reviews Staging After Fan Complaints About Obstructed Views in Amsterdam
item.description: Fans attending the opening shows of Harry Styles’ new ‘Together, Together’ tour in Amsterdam have voiced concerns over obstructed views from certain premium floor sections. The issues, which surfaced after the first two performances at Johan Cruyff Arena on May 16 and 17, 2026, stem from the height of the stage, equipment, and elaborate production setup. Social media clips highlighted difficulties for some concertgoers in seeing the performance clearly, sparking discussions about staging design and expectations for high-priced tickets. In response, a tour representative told Billboard U.K. that specific areas of the staging are under careful review and will be adjusted where possible while adhering to safety regulations. The floor concept was intentionally designed to allow freedom of movement and flexible viewing positions rather than fixed spots, aligning with Styles’ established live show philosophy. Despite the complaints, many attendees praised the ambitious two-hour production, w

item.title: Stephen Colbert Plans Post-'Late Show' Projects Including a 'Lord of the Rings' Film
item.description: Stephen Colbert is preparing to conclude his tenure as host of 'The Late Show' on CBS, a role famously held by David Letterman. While the exact details of his next career moves remain flexible, Colbert has revealed that he is co-writing a new 'Lord of the Rings' movie for Warner Bros., collaborating with his son, Peter McGee, and director Peter Jackson. The film will adapt six chapters from 'The Fellowship of the Ring' that were not included in the original trilogy. Colbert has expressed interest in continuing in television but has not confirmed a specific project yet, emphasizing that he enjoys creating and collaborating. He has also received various offers for new work, which he has postponed considering until after finishing 'The Late Show.' Despite fan speculation, Colbert has ruled out pursuing a comedy tour and running for public office, including the presidency, labeling the latter as unrealistic. Before embarking on his next professional chapter, Colbert plans to spend time wit

item.title: Paris Audience Poses With Dalmatian Dogs After 1961 Screening of Disney Classic
item.description: On December 14, 1961, Parisian spectators attended a screening of Walt Disney’s animated classic 'One Hundred and One Dalmatians' on the Champs-Elysees, an event that drew notable attention for its unique photo opportunity. In the aftermath of the screening, attendees, some wearing masks, posed alongside Dalmatian dogs, reflecting the excitement surrounding the film’s release. Based on Dodie Smith’s children’s novel, the movie was groundbreaking for its time, becoming the first animated film to earn $10 million at the box office. Over the decades, including various reissues, it has become one of the highest-grossing animated features of all time. The 1961 Paris event exemplifies the cultural impact of Disney films in Europe during the early 1960s, highlighting the combination of cinematic innovation and public engagement that helped solidify the film’s legendary status. The photograph of masked spectators with the Dalmatians captures a moment of joyful interaction between the audience

item.title: Kim Kardashian Discusses Taking Approximately 35 Supplements Daily on Podcast
item.description: Kim Kardashian shared details about her extensive daily supplement routine during an appearance on Amy Poehler’s podcast 'Good Hang' on May 19. The 45-year-old reality star disclosed that she consumes around 35 supplements each day, dividing them into three separate intakes to manage the regimen more effectively. Kardashian admitted that the routine can sometimes feel overwhelming, especially when dealing with large capsules such as fish oil pills, which she described as particularly challenging to swallow. She even mentioned experiencing 'pill fatigue' and temporarily stopping the fish oil supplements at one point. However, after reviewing her blood work results, which clearly showed the negative impact of halting them, she decided to resume the full regimen. Kardashian emphasized the importance of the supplements for her overall health and wellness, noting that the blood tests provided convincing evidence to continue. In a lighthearted moment, she joked about wishing for a simpler so

item.title: Stephen Colbert Reveals Unused Graphics as 'Late Show' Nears End
item.description: Stephen Colbert began the final week of his nearly 11-year tenure on 'The Late Show' by showcasing previously unseen graphics and sketches that never aired. In a segment titled 'Graphics Graveyard,' named after a Slack channel where the show's staff discarded unused ideas, Colbert revealed a graphic declaring Hillary Clinton the winner of the 2016 presidential election—an image that ultimately did not make it to broadcast. Other unaired graphics included a satirical Thanksgiving magazine called Giblets and a pun-filled Martha Stewart Living cover reading 'Donner party or dinner party.' Colbert's farewell week will feature high-profile guests such as Jon Stewart, Steven Spielberg, David Byrne, and Bruce Springsteen. The finale’s programming remains partially undisclosed. CBS’s decision to end the show has been controversial, with critics suggesting political motives linked to Trump amid Paramount Global’s Skydance Media merger, although the network maintains the decision was financial,

item.title: GOP Declines to Fund Trump-Endorsed Texas Candidate Amid Controversy
item.description: Following his recent primary victories, former President Donald Trump's influence in the Republican Party has faced pushback with his endorsement of Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton. Paxton, who has faced multiple scandals, is now likely to be the Republican nominee for the U.S. Senate seat currently held by John Cornyn. However, GOP leadership has made it clear that they will not allocate party funds for Paxton's general election campaign. Conservative journalist David Drucker reported on MS NOW's “Morning Joe” that the party views Paxton as Trump's responsibility due to Trump's considerable personal political resources, which range between $350 million and $400 million. The party plans to prioritize funding for other races in states like North Carolina and Maine instead. This move highlights the tension within the Republican Party between Trump's influence and the establishment's strategic priorities, as they attempt to balance high-profile endorsements with overall campaign resourc

item.title: Key Races and Political Stakes in Tuesday’s Primaries Across Six States
item.description: On May 19, 2026, six states—Alabama, Georgia, Idaho, Kentucky, Oregon, and Pennsylvania—hold primaries, making it one of the busiest days in the election calendar. The spotlight is on Kentucky, where President Donald Trump is targeting Rep. Thomas Massie in a highly expensive and symbolic House primary, reflecting Trump’s ongoing effort to challenge GOP members who have opposed him. Meanwhile, in Georgia, Governor Brian Kemp’s endorsement of political newcomer Derek Dooley for Senate will test Kemp’s influence against Trump-aligned candidates, likely leading to a June 16 runoff. Other notable contests include Kentucky Rep. Andy Barr aiming for Mitch McConnell’s Senate seat, Georgia Lt. Gov. Burt Jones in a tight gubernatorial primary, and Alabama’s Rep. Barry Moore facing a competitive Senate race. Democrats are also aiming to flip two state Supreme Court seats in Georgia, signaling potential trends for future elections. Pennsylvania Governor Josh Shapiro’s reelection campaign seeks to

item.title: Google Launches Gemini Omni AI for Conversational Video and Media Editing
item.description: At Google I/O 2026, Google unveiled Gemini Omni, a new multimodal artificial intelligence system designed to simplify creative workflows across video, images, audio, and text. Gemini Omni allows users to edit and generate media using natural language, either through voice or text commands, eliminating the need for traditional complex editing software. Demonstrations at the conference showcased users transforming ordinary video scenes into cinematic experiences, altering backgrounds, generating sound effects, and refining projects with simple conversational prompts. Google emphasized that the system is 'omni-modal,' meaning it can handle multiple media types within a single workflow. The first version, Gemini Omni Flash, is expected to roll out around September 2026, although official pricing has not yet been announced. Some advanced creative features may be limited to premium or enterprise subscriptions. This launch highlights Google’s ongoing push to integrate AI more deeply into its

item.title: Behind the Scenes with Brigitte Bardot in 'Babette Goes to War' (1959)
item.description: The portraits of Brigitte Bardot taken during the filming of 'Babette Goes to War' in 1959 provide a unique glimpse into the French actress at a pivotal moment in her career. Known primarily for her image as a 1950s sex symbol, Bardot is shown in military-inspired costumes while maintaining her signature beehive hairstyle, a look that would go on to influence global fashion trends. The photographs capture both candid moments of laughter and carefully posed promotional shots, revealing a playful and rebellious side of Bardot that challenged conventional ideas of femininity during that era. This collection not only documents the height of 'Bardot-mania' but also reflects the cinematic style and culture of the late 1950s, highlighting the evolution of celebrity imagery and the era’s approach to glamour and publicity. These portraits act as a cultural time capsule, celebrating both Bardot’s star power and the broader fashion and film trends of the decade.

item.title: Jefferson and Madison’s Views on Religious Freedom and Government
item.description: On May 17, 2026, thousands of Americans gathered at the National Mall for 'Rededicate 250,' a prayer rally aimed at honoring the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary. Led by U.S. House Speaker Mike Johnson and other conservative figures, the event sought to 'rededicate the United States of America as one nation under God.' While the rally drew political and religious attention, it also sparked criticism for blurring the lines between church and state. Historical analysis shows that Founding Fathers Thomas Jefferson and James Madison championed religious freedom and strict separation of church and state. Jefferson authored the Virginia Bill for Religious Freedom, ensuring liberty of conscience and protection from government interference in religious affairs. Madison defended similar principles in his 'Memorial and Remonstrance Against Religious Assessments,' opposing government funding for religious instruction. Both men were committed to ensuring broad religious liberties as es

item.title: Trump's Primary Endorsements and Policy Priorities Spark Internal GOP Concerns Ahead of 2026 Midterms
item.description: According to a NOTUS report, President Donald Trump is prioritizing personal and loyalist-focused decisions that some Republicans fear could jeopardize the party's narrow majorities in the House and Senate during the 2026 midterm elections. With less than six months until voting, Trump has endorsed controversial figures such as Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton over longtime Sen. John Cornyn, despite significant party resources already invested in Cornyn's reelection. This move has strained relations within the Senate GOP, particularly after Cornyn's history of supporting Trump, including his role as whip during the first Trump administration. Republicans worry the primary fight will drain funds needed for competitive races in battleground states. Additionally, Trump continues to push for a $1 billion White House ballroom renovation, which has complicated funding discussions for Secret Service operations and drawn criticism amid public concerns over inflation and rising costs. The admi

item.title: Lahore Restores Historical Names and Heritage Sites to Revive Cultural Legacy
item.description: Lahore, one of Pakistan's major cities, is undergoing a significant cultural restoration project nearly 80 years after the partition. The Lahore Heritage Area Revival (LHAR) initiative, launched in 2025 under Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz and initially envisioned by former Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif, aims to restore historical Hindu, Sikh, Jain, and colonial-era names to the city's streets and neighborhoods. Key changes include reverting Islampura to Krishan Nagar, Rehman Gali to Ram Gali, and Sunnat Nagar to Sant Nagar, among others. Colonial names like Lawrence Gardens and Queens Road have also been reinstated. Beyond street renaming, the project includes restoring heritage sites such as Minto Park (Greater Iqbal Park), historical cricket grounds, and a traditional wrestling arena known for legends like Gama Pehalwan. This initiative seeks to boost heritage tourism, improve Pakistan’s global image, and attract diplomatic and economic support while signaling social stability to interna

item.title: Geraldine Fitzgerald: Celebrating the Legacy of a Pioneering Irish Actress
item.description: Geraldine Fitzgerald was a celebrated Irish actress whose talent and emotional depth left an indelible mark on Hollywood's Golden Age. Best known for her unforgettable portrayal of Isabella Linton in the 1939 classic 'Wuthering Heights,' Fitzgerald earned an Academy Award nomination for Best Supporting Actress. Unlike many of her peers, she prioritized artistic integrity over studio pressures, which set her apart as an independent and respected figure in the entertainment industry. Beyond film, she had a distinguished Broadway career and later became a pioneer in street theater, demonstrating her enduring commitment to storytelling in its many forms. This article also showcases portraits of Fitzgerald from the late 1930s and 1940s, capturing the elegance and charm of her early career. Her dedication to her craft and her influence on stage and screen continue to inspire actors and audiences alike.

item.title: Gene Laurents: Iconic 1960s Fashion Photography for Vogue and Harper’s Bazaar
item.description: Gene Laurents was an American photographer celebrated for his elegant and cinematic style in mid-20th century fashion photography. Active primarily in the 1950s and 1960s, he contributed extensively to leading publications such as Vogue and Harper’s Bazaar.
